rows.title: Food preparation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable kitchen environments, which are difficult for AI to automate directly. While AI might optimize inventory management or scheduling, the primary tasks of washing, cutting, and preparing food remain resistant to digital replacement, though some labor may be displaced by industrial food processing automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of food preparation—chopping, mixing, and cleaning—are highly repetitive and increasingly automatable via specialized robotics, leading to high agentic potential. While kitchens are physically dynamic, the tasks themselves are procedural and require minimal high-level cognitive synthesis or human-to-human emotional labor, making them prime candidates for replacement by autonomous systems.

rows.title: Food processing equipment workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as loading ingredients, cleaning machinery, and manual quality checks, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize recipes, monitor sensor data, and automate some equipment controls, the requirement for human presence in a physical manufacturing environment to handle unpredictable mechanical issues and sanitation limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of this role—monitoring gauges, mixing ingredients, and operating machinery—are highly susceptible to automation, as the output is a physical product generated through repeatable, rule-based processes. While the environment involves physical labor, it is a controlled industrial setting where AI-integrated robotics can easily replace human intervention in loading, monitoring, and adjusting production parameters.

rows.title: Painting and coating workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and presence in industrial or repair environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. While AI and computer vision can enhance automated spray systems and quality control sensors, the need for human workers to set up equipment, prepare surfaces, and perform manual touch-ups in unpredictable repair settings remains significant.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is a physical coating application, which is already highly automated in industrial settings and represents a high-potential target for robotic agents. While some manual touch-up work exists, the tasks are largely procedural and repetitive, requiring minimal high-level cognitive synthesis or human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Semiconductor processing technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, requiring presence in cleanrooms to handle wafers, maintain equipment, and perform manual inspections. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of monitoring process data, optimizing machine parameters, and performing high-precision quality control, which automates the information-processing and monitoring aspects of the job.
rows.new_rationale: The work occurs in a highly controlled, sterile cleanroom environment where tasks are increasingly automated and data-driven, making the physical manipulation of wafers highly susceptible to full robotic integration. While technicians provide critical monitoring and maintenance, the core output is the production of digital artifacts (microchips) within a rigid, predictable system that requires minimal human-to-human interaction or subjective moral judgment.

rows.title: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as clearing tables, carrying heavy trays, and cleaning, which are difficult to automate with current robotics. However, AI is increasingly handling digital peripheral tasks like order-taking via kiosks or voice-AI drive-thrus and managing reservations, which reduces the need for human staff in front-of-house roles.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical environment of a restaurant involves some variability, the core tasks—taking orders, clearing tables, and serving—are increasingly automatable via kiosks, mobile apps, and robotic delivery systems. The role requires minimal complex synthesis, and while human interaction is a component of the service experience, it is largely functional rather than a deep, irreplaceable human-to-human bond.

rows.title: Retail sales workers
rows.old_rationale: Retail sales involve a mix of physical tasks (stocking shelves, demonstrating products) and interpersonal interaction that provides a barrier to full automation. However, AI is increasingly handling the information-heavy aspects of the job, such as personalized product recommendations, answering technical questions via kiosks/mobile apps, and automating inventory management and checkout processes, which reduces the total human labor required per store.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of retail sales—product recommendation, inventory management, and payment processing—are highly digitizable and already being displaced by e-commerce and automated kiosks. While human interaction provides a marginal 'service' value, the tasks are largely routine and occur in highly controlled, predictable physical environments, leaving little room for complex, non-delegatable accountability.

rows.title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly automate back-end knowledge work such as lesson planning, grading, and administrative communication, which currently occupy a large portion of a teacher's time. However, the core of the role involves physical supervision, emotional support, and real-time behavioral management of young children, which creates a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves managing the highly unpredictable, physical, and emotional environment of a classroom, which requires real-time human intervention. The pedagogical value is deeply rooted in the human-to-human bond and the non-delegatable ethical responsibility for the safety and development of children, making full automation impossible.

rows.title: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rows.old_rationale: While AI and autonomous vehicle technology are advancing, the core of this job involves significant physical labor, such as loading/unloading cargo and navigating unpredictable 'last-mile' environments. AI may optimize routing and assist with sales communication, but the physical requirement to move goods from a vehicle to a customer's door remains a major barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of this role—transporting goods from point A to point B—is highly susceptible to automation via autonomous vehicles and robotic logistics, which can execute the entire delivery chain. While the physical environment (traffic, unstructured delivery sites) presents challenges, these are increasingly manageable through sensor fusion and AI, leaving little room for human-specific cognitive or emotional necessity beyond basic customer interaction.

rows.title: High school teachers
rows.old_rationale: High school teachers are heavily exposed because core digital tasks like lesson planning, content creation, and grading are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the physical requirement of classroom management and interpersonal mentorship provides a buffer, AI's ability to provide personalized tutoring and automated feedback significantly reshapes the instructional delivery and administrative workload of the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can automate lesson planning and grading, the core value of teaching is deeply rooted in human mentorship, emotional regulation, and the management of unpredictable, high-stakes social dynamics in a classroom. The physical presence and moral authority required to manage adolescent development and ensure student safety create a high barrier to full automation, as the 'output' is the holistic growth of a human being rather than a digital artifact.

rows.title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires presence on a factory floor to handle materials and maintain machinery. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of handling the digital aspects of the job, such as CNC programming, machine monitoring, and quality inspection, which is already contributing to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is the physical production of parts based on digital blueprints or programs, which is highly susceptible to automation via CNC and robotics. While the environment involves heavy machinery, it is a controlled factory setting where the primary tasks—monitoring, loading, and basic adjustments—are increasingly handled by integrated software and automated systems.

rows.title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including manual dexterity, machine setup, and material handling in a factory environment. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of optimizing CNC programming, interpreting CAD designs, and monitoring machine health, which increases worker productivity and reduces the demand for traditional tool and die making skills.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is increasingly defined by digital CAD/CAM files and CNC machine instructions, which are highly susceptible to automation. While the work requires high-level technical synthesis to troubleshoot physical tolerances, the environment is largely controlled and industrial, and the value is derived from the precision of the physical object rather than human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Musicians and singers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality digital music and vocals, a significant portion of this occupation involves live performance, physical presence at venues, and human-centric events like religious services and concerts. The 'human element' and the physical act of playing an instrument or singing in real-time provide a natural barrier, though AI will increasingly compete for studio/session work and background music production.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality musical artifacts (agentic potential), the core value of a musician is deeply rooted in the human-to-human connection of live performance and the cultural resonance of a human persona. The work environment is generally controlled (stages, studios), and the lack of systemic liability means the occupation is protected more by the human desire for authentic human expression than by technical or legal barriers.

rows.title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital tasks (writing case reports, risk assessment, and data-driven monitoring) and low-exposure physical/interpersonal tasks (home visits, drug testing, and building trust with hostile clients). While AI can significantly automate documentation and predictive risk modeling, the legal requirement for human testimony and the physical necessity of community supervision provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While reporting and monitoring can be digitized, the core of this role involves high-stakes, real-time physical interventions in volatile environments where human presence is required for de-escalation and rapport. The legal and ethical weight of life-altering decisions regarding an individual's liberty creates a non-delegatable accountability that necessitates human judgment and moral authority.

rows.title: Dancers and choreographers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, athleticism, and real-time human interaction in physical spaces, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist choreographers with music selection or administrative tasks, the fundamental value of the work lies in the human body's movement and live artistic expression.
rows.new_rationale: While choreography can be digitally simulated, the core value of dance is rooted in the physical, human-to-human connection and the visceral experience of a live performer, which AI cannot replicate. The occupation requires high-level creative synthesis to interpret and express complex human emotions, but it lacks the systemic liability or high-stakes physical unpredictability that would necessitate human oversight in a critical infrastructure context.

rows.title: Nuclear engineers
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear engineering is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—such as reactor design, dose calculations, and safety simulations—are performed using digital tools and complex mathematical modeling. While the high-stakes regulatory environment and the need for physical inspections at power plants provide a buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in its ability to optimize designs, analyze sensor data for predictive maintenance, and automate technical documentation.
rows.new_rationale: While nuclear engineering involves high-level digital modeling and simulation, the physical implementation and maintenance of nuclear systems in volatile, high-stakes environments require human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to software. The extreme safety requirements and catastrophic potential of failure necessitate a human 'buck stops here' accountability that remains legally and ethically non-transferable to autonomous agents.

rows.title: General office clerks
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data entry, document formatting, scheduling, and information processing—are almost entirely digital and routine, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While some physical tasks like handling mail or greeting visitors remain, the vast majority of the workload can be absorbed by AI agents and automated software, as reflected in the BLS's projected decline for the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by general office clerks—data entry, scheduling, filing, and document processing—are digital artifacts that can be fully executed by software agents. Because the work occurs in highly controlled, predictable office environments and requires minimal complex synthesis or human-centric emotional labor, it is exceptionally vulnerable to total automation.

rows.title: Janitors and building cleaners
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor in unpredictable and varied environments like schools, hospitals, and construction sites. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like supply ordering or scheduling, the primary work of cleaning, disinfecting, and making physical repairs remains a significant barrier to AI and robotic automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the tasks are highly routine, the agentic output potential is low because the value is derived from physical manipulation of unstructured, messy environments rather than digital artifacts. The primary barrier to automation is the high degree of physical unpredictability in navigating varied, non-standardized building layouts and performing manual repairs, which remains difficult for current autonomous agents to navigate reliably.

rows.title: Waiters and waitresses
rows.old_rationale: While AI and automation are increasingly handling digital tasks like order-taking and payments via kiosks or mobile apps, the core of the job remains physical and interpersonal. The physical requirements of carrying trays, clearing tables, and providing real-time human hospitality in a dynamic environment create a significant barrier to full AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical delivery of food and table maintenance requires robotics, the core 'agentic' tasks of ordering and payment are already being digitized and offloaded to kiosks and apps. The role retains a moderate score in human necessity due to the social expectation of hospitality and service rapport, though the cognitive demands are low and the environment, while physically active, is largely structured and predictable.

rows.title: Grounds maintenance workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier against AI. While autonomous mowers or drones may assist with specific tasks, the majority of the work—such as planting, tree climbing, and complex manual maintenance—requires human dexterity and physical presence that AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of the environment, which remains highly resistant to automation due to the extreme variability of outdoor settings, weather, and biological growth. While AI can assist in planning, the 'agentic output' is limited because the final execution requires complex, real-time physical navigation of unstructured, high-stakes environments that current robotics cannot reliably manage.

rows.title: Financial clerks
rows.old_rationale: Financial clerks perform routine, digital-first tasks such as data entry, record updating, and basic financial calculations that are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some roles involve customer interaction, the core functions—processing claims, verifying credit, and managing payroll—are increasingly handled by AI-driven software, leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by financial clerks involve digital data entry, record reconciliation, and transaction processing, all of which are highly susceptible to automation via software agents. Because the work occurs in highly structured office environments and relies on predetermined rules and protocols, there is minimal requirement for human physical presence or complex, non-routine cognitive synthesis.

rows.title: Lawyers
rows.old_rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving document drafting, legal research, and complex data analysis—all areas where LLMs excel. While courtroom advocacy and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation provide a protective barrier, the vast majority of billable hours are spent on tasks that AI can now significantly accelerate or automate, leading to major structural changes in the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the legal output (contracts, filings, research) is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation, the role remains anchored by the requirement for human moral agency and professional liability. The legal system is fundamentally built on human trust, advocacy, and the 'buck-stops-here' accountability of a licensed attorney, which cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent regardless of its cognitive synthesis capabilities.

rows.title: Bartenders
rows.old_rationale: The core of bartending involves physical manipulation of objects, manual cleaning, and real-time social interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory management or automated ordering, the primary value of the role lies in human presence, physical service, and the social 'hospitality' aspect which remains difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical act of mixing drinks is highly automatable, the role is heavily defined by the 'third place' social environment, requiring real-time human judgment for intoxication monitoring and conflict de-escalation. The high systemic accountability stems from the legal liability associated with serving alcohol, which requires a human agent to exercise moral and legal authority that cannot be fully offloaded to software.

rows.title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, dressing, and wound care, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. However, AI will significantly impact the information-processing aspects of the job, including documenting patient care, monitoring vitals, and assisting with diagnostic reporting, leading to moderate overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While LPNs perform some routine data entry, the core of the role involves physical, high-stakes interaction with patients in unpredictable environments (homes, care facilities) that require real-time physical dexterity and human presence. The necessity for human empathy, physical touch, and the legal/ethical accountability for life-critical patient care makes this role highly resistant to full automation, despite the structured nature of some medical tasks.

rows.title: Middle school teachers
rows.old_rationale: Middle school teachers have high exposure because a significant portion of their workload—lesson planning, grading, and content creation—is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for classroom management, emotional support for adolescents, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high agentic potential for AI, the core of the role involves real-time, high-stakes social and emotional navigation of adolescent development in an unstructured, volatile classroom environment. The necessity for human presence, moral authority, and the legal/ethical responsibility for the safety and development of minors makes the role fundamentally resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Quality control inspectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical inspection and data analysis. While AI-powered computer vision systems are rapidly automating the visual detection of defects, the role still requires significant physical presence to handle materials, operate manual gauges, and perform mechanical tests that robots cannot yet fully replicate in diverse manufacturing environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of inspection, measurement, and reporting are highly susceptible to automation via computer vision and sensor-integrated robotics, which can execute the 'accept/reject' decision loop autonomously. While the work occurs in physical manufacturing environments, the tasks themselves are largely procedural and data-driven, requiring minimal human-to-human interaction or complex, non-routine synthesis that an AI agent could not replicate.

rows.title: Pharmacy technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as measuring medications, packaging prescriptions, and interacting with patients in person, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI and automated dispensing systems are highly capable of handling the information-processing tasks like insurance claims, inventory management, and prescription data entry, which constitute a large portion of the daily workload.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of pharmacy technicians—data entry, inventory management, and prescription labeling—are highly susceptible to automation via software and robotics. While the environment is relatively controlled, the high-stakes nature of medication safety imposes a significant legal and ethical accountability burden that requires human oversight, even if the physical and cognitive labor is increasingly delegated to autonomous systems.

rows.title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-level knowledge work and intense interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like documentation, treatment planning, and monitoring progress, the core of the job requires human empathy, crisis intervention, and the navigation of complex emotional and physical environments that AI cannot yet replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with documentation and data-driven treatment planning, the core value of this role is rooted in the therapeutic alliance, empathy, and the human-to-human connection, which cannot be delegated to software. The work involves high-stakes, unpredictable crisis intervention and ethical accountability for life-altering decisions, requiring a level of moral and legal responsibility that remains fundamentally human.

rows.title: Social and human service assistants
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital administrative work and high-touch interpersonal service. AI can significantly automate the 'knowledge work' components, such as researching community resources, assisting with complex benefit applications, and coordinating service schedules. However, the core of the job requires physical presence in homes or clinics and deep emotional intelligence to support clients in crisis, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like paperwork and resource matching have high agentic potential, the core of this role involves navigating volatile, high-stakes human environments where physical presence and emotional rapport are essential. The necessity for human-to-human connection and the ethical weight of managing vulnerable populations' lives create a high barrier to full automation, as the 'product' is fundamentally rooted in trust and human intervention.

rows.title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of high-level knowledge work, such as diagnosing conditions and analyzing test results, which is highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for exams, procedures (like delivering babies or administering anesthesia), and complex interpersonal communication that provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics and monitoring, the core value of this role is tied to high-stakes physical interventions in volatile clinical environments where real-time, non-routine physical adjustments are required. The necessity for human presence, empathy, and the assumption of legal and ethical liability for life-altering decisions makes this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Civil engineers
rows.old_rationale: Civil engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, including CAD design, data analysis, and cost estimation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role is anchored by the necessity for physical site inspections, real-world surveying, and the legal accountability of a Professional Engineer (PE) license, which requires human judgment and liability that AI cannot currently replace.
rows.new_rationale: While civil engineers use digital design tools, their output is fundamentally tied to physical, high-stakes infrastructure that must interact with unpredictable, site-specific geological and environmental variables. The requirement for professional licensure and the legal liability for public safety create a non-delegatable 'buck stops here' responsibility that AI cannot assume, regardless of its cognitive synthesis capabilities.

rows.title: Clinical laboratory technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI and computer vision are rapidly automating the interpretation of slides and test results (high exposure), the physical requirements of preparing specimens, maintaining complex hardware, and interacting with patients provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is diagnostic data, which is inherently digital and increasingly generated by automated, AI-integrated laboratory systems. While the environment is highly controlled and standardized, the role retains high systemic accountability due to the life-critical nature of diagnostic accuracy, requiring human oversight to validate machine-generated results and maintain legal liability.

rows.title: Painters, construction and maintenance
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity, mobility at heights, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable physical environments. While AI might assist with peripheral business tasks like estimating or scheduling, it cannot perform the labor-intensive tasks of surface preparation and paint application.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this job is fundamentally physical, requiring complex manipulation of tools in highly unstructured, variable, and often hazardous environments that remain difficult for current robotics to navigate. While cognitive tasks like color matching or surface assessment exist, the core value is the physical application of coatings, which is resistant to digital agentic execution.

rows.title: Elementary, middle, and high school principals
rows.old_rationale: Principals perform significant knowledge work—such as curriculum development, budget management, and data analysis of student performance—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. However, the core of the role requires physical presence for school safety, real-time crisis management, and high-stakes interpersonal interactions with students, parents, and staff that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While principals manage data and schedules that could be optimized by AI, the core of the role involves navigating the highly unpredictable, emotionally charged, and socially complex environment of a school. The necessity for human moral authority, crisis management, and the legal/ethical accountability for the safety and development of minors makes this role fundamentally resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Bakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of a baker are physical and sensory, involving manual dexterity for shaping dough and real-time monitoring of physical products in high-heat environments. While AI can optimize recipes or assist with inventory management and scheduling, the fundamental work of kneading, decorating, and handling equipment remains a physical task resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: While baking involves standardized processes that are increasingly automated in industrial settings, the core output remains a physical, perishable product that requires manual manipulation of dough and real-time sensory adjustments. The physical environment of a bakery—involving heat, heavy lifting, and manual dexterity—presents significant hurdles for full autonomous agent replacement, keeping the agentic potential low despite the routine nature of many tasks.

rows.title: Industrial production managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical oversight. AI will significantly enhance the analytical aspects of the job, such as production scheduling, budget optimization, and predictive maintenance data analysis, but the core requirements of physical plant presence, personnel management, and real-time problem-solving in a manual environment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While production managers utilize data-driven software, their output is tethered to the physical, unpredictable reality of a factory floor where mechanical failures and supply chain volatility require real-time, non-digital intervention. The role demands high-level human leadership for conflict resolution and safety oversight, carrying significant legal and ethical liability for workplace accidents and product quality that cannot be fully offloaded to autonomous systems.

rows.title: Social and community service managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as grant writing, data analysis, and budget management, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core responsibilities of community stakeholder engagement, staff supervision, and navigating complex human crises provide a buffer that requires physical presence and high-level emotional intelligence.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like reporting and budgeting have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves navigating complex, volatile human crises and community dynamics that require high-level synthesis and human presence. The necessity for moral authority, trust-building, and ethical accountability in high-stakes social interventions makes the human element non-delegatable, despite the potential for AI to assist in data-driven decision-making.

rows.title: Architectural and engineering managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and digital, involving project planning, budgeting, and technical oversight that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and physical site visits, the core tasks of analyzing technical data, scheduling, and coordinating complex engineering workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the planning and scheduling can be digitized, the role requires high-level synthesis of complex, non-standardized physical projects where the 'final output' is a safe, functional, and compliant physical structure or product. The high score in systemic accountability and human necessity reflects the reality that these managers bear ultimate legal and ethical responsibility for life-critical infrastructure, requiring human judgment to navigate the ambiguity of physical site constraints and team dynamics that AI cannot yet fully own.

rows.title: Psychologists
rows.old_rationale: Psychology is a knowledge-based profession where core tasks like analyzing data, writing reports, and researching are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the critical requirements for human empathy, ethical judgment, and real-time interpersonal observation in clinical and school settings create a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize diagnostic data and suggest treatment plans, the core value of psychology is rooted in the therapeutic alliance and the human-to-human connection, which cannot be delegated to software. The high stakes of mental health and behavioral intervention require human moral authority and legal accountability that an autonomous agent cannot possess.

rows.title: Bill and account collectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, consisting of data analysis, skip tracing, and communication via phone or computer. AI and automated systems are already driving a projected 10% decline in employment by handling routine notifications and payment processing, while advanced LLMs are increasingly capable of managing the negotiation and customer service aspects of debt collection.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly susceptible to automation because the output is entirely digital (data updates, payment processing, and communication logs) and occurs within a strictly controlled, software-mediated environment. While human empathy is cited as a skill, the core function is transactional and rule-bound, making it a prime candidate for autonomous agents that can navigate regulatory compliance and negotiation scripts without physical or complex cognitive constraints.

rows.title: Medical scientists
rows.old_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can accelerate data analysis and hypothesis generation, the core value of medical science remains rooted in the physical execution of clinical trials and the high-stakes, non-delegatable ethical responsibility for human safety and regulatory compliance. The requirement for novel, multi-variable synthesis in biological research remains a human-led endeavor, as the physical, unpredictable nature of biological systems and the legal liability for life-altering medical outcomes prevent full automation.

rows.title: Gambling services workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical hospitality and digital-adjacent information processing. While AI and automation are significantly impacting sports book writers and slot operations through online platforms and automated betting, the core of the job for dealers and managers involves physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time human interaction to ensure a 'pleasant experience' and monitor for physical rule violations.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of gambling services—managing bets, tracking game states, and processing payouts—are highly digitizable and already being replaced by online platforms. While the physical environment of a casino requires human presence for security, conflict de-escalation, and regulatory compliance, the actual 'work' of the game is increasingly algorithmic, leaving the human role primarily as a social and regulatory interface.

rows.title: Drywall installers, ceiling tile installers, and tapers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual labor, dexterity, and movement in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or material estimation, it cannot perform the physical installation, cutting, or finishing of drywall and ceiling tiles.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this job is entirely physical, requiring the manipulation of heavy materials in unstructured, variable construction environments that are difficult for current robotics to navigate. While the cognitive tasks are routine and procedural, the primary barrier to automation is the physical dexterity and spatial adaptability required to work in non-standardized, real-world building sites.

rows.title: Flooring installers and tile and stone setters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual dexterity, heavy lifting, and real-time adaptation to irregular physical surfaces in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks or design visualization, it cannot perform the tactile labor of cutting, laying, and finishing materials on-site.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, variable environments (e.g., uneven subfloors, remodeling constraints) that require real-time tactile adjustment. Because the output is a physical installation rather than a digital artifact, the agentic potential for AI is extremely low, as current robotics lack the dexterity and environmental adaptability to replace human workers in these volatile, non-standardized spaces.

rows.title: Natural sciences managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving data analysis, budgeting, and technical reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While the job requires significant human-centric leadership and physical oversight of laboratories, AI will drastically increase productivity in reviewing research, drafting operational reports, and optimizing resource allocation.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves high-level strategic synthesis and complex decision-making, the output is deeply tied to human team management, ethical oversight of scientific research, and physical laboratory safety, which limits pure agentic automation. The necessity for human leadership, conflict resolution, and legal accountability for high-stakes scientific outcomes makes this role highly resistant to full replacement by autonomous agents.

rows.title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of administrative knowledge work and high-stakes physical presence. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like budgeting, scheduling, and curriculum planning, the core of the job involves physical facility oversight, staff supervision, and building trust-based relationships with parents and children that cannot be replicated by AI.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like budgeting and scheduling have moderate agentic potential, the core of the role involves managing the volatile, high-stakes physical environment of childcare where human presence and emotional intelligence are non-negotiable. The systemic accountability for child safety and regulatory compliance creates a 'human-in-the-loop' requirement that cannot be delegated to software, as the primary value is the trust and care provided to families.

rows.title: Opticians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work such as measuring faces, adjusting frames with tools, and teaching patients how to insert contact lenses. While AI can automate administrative tasks and assist in frame selection via virtual try-on technology, the core requirements of physical fitting, manual repairs, and real-time interpersonal healthcare guidance provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical adjustment of frames requires manual dexterity, the core value of the role—measuring, selecting, and ordering eyewear—is increasingly digitized and can be largely automated via computer vision and AI-driven retail interfaces. The human necessity remains moderate, primarily centered on customer service and the physical comfort of fitting, but the technical aspects of the job are highly susceptible to agentic replacement.

rows.title: Interpreters and translators
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is purely digital information (text and speech) in a domain where Large Language Models already demonstrate near-human proficiency. While high-stakes interpreting (legal, medical) and sign language still require human presence and nuanced judgment, the vast majority of translation and routine interpreting tasks are being rapidly automated or shifted to 'post-editing' roles, significantly reducing the total human labor required per unit of work.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is linguistic data, which is highly susceptible to automation via AI agents, especially in written translation and increasingly in real-time speech synthesis. While the work requires high-level cultural and contextual synthesis, the human necessity remains high primarily in high-stakes, sensitive environments like courtrooms or medical settings where legal liability and the need for human-mediated trust are critical.

rows.title: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital design, with AI and automation already driving a projected decline in dental technician roles. While the physical assembly, polishing, and repair of medical devices require manual dexterity, the 'knowledge' phase of the job—interpreting prescriptions and designing appliances—is rapidly shifting toward AI-integrated CAD/CAM software and 3D printing.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly susceptible to automation because the output is a physical object derived from digital CAD/CAM files and 3D printing, which are increasingly autonomous. While the environment is a controlled lab setting, the role requires precision and adherence to medical prescriptions, placing the primary accountability on the technician to ensure the device fits the patient's specific anatomy.

rows.title: Concierges
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of digital information processing and physical hospitality. While AI can automate core tasks like making reservations, providing local recommendations, and answering emails, the job still requires a physical presence for greeting guests, handling packages, and providing high-touch interpersonal service that is central to the luxury and residential experience.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of a concierge's tasks—booking, scheduling, and information retrieval—are digital artifacts that can be fully executed by autonomous agents, leading to high agentic potential. While the role requires human-to-human interaction for hospitality and comfort, the core functional tasks are highly susceptible to automation, and the work environment is generally a controlled, predictable indoor setting.

rows.title: Logging workers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving the operation of heavy machinery and manual labor in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI may improve peripheral tasks like log grading through computer vision or optimize harvest planning, the physical requirements of felling and transporting timber provide a strong barrier to AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: While logging involves significant mechanical operation, the output is a physical transformation of the environment that requires navigating highly unstructured, volatile, and dangerous terrain that remains difficult for autonomous agents to master. The core value is physical labor and environmental interaction rather than digital synthesis or human-to-human connection, making it resistant to full automation despite the high degree of mechanization.

rows.title: Medical transcriptionists
rows.old_rationale: Medical transcription is a purely digital, routine information-processing task that has already been heavily impacted by speech recognition. Modern AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) can now handle complex medical terminology, formatting, and summarization with high accuracy, transforming the human role into a diminishing oversight function or eliminating it entirely.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this role is entirely digital and already heavily mediated by speech-to-text software, making it a prime candidate for full automation. Because the work occurs in a controlled, virtual environment and involves standardized medical documentation, there is minimal requirement for human physical presence or complex, non-routine synthesis.

rows.title: Private detectives and investigators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of digital research and physical field work. AI significantly enhances the digital aspects—such as background checks, social media analysis, and report writing—making investigators much more productive, but the core requirements of physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and undercover store work provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital data gathering and report generation are highly automatable, the role requires physical surveillance in unpredictable, unstructured environments and the ability to navigate complex legal and ethical boundaries. The necessity for human judgment in interviewing, maintaining chain-of-custody for evidence, and assuming legal liability for investigative actions creates a significant barrier to full AI replacement.

rows.title: Environmental engineers
rows.old_rationale: Environmental engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, system design, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is insulated by the necessity for physical site inspections, regulatory compliance monitoring in the field, and the legal accountability associated with Professional Engineer (PE) licensure.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate designs and analyze data, the final output often requires physical implementation in volatile, unstructured environments like contaminated sites or complex municipal infrastructure. The role carries high systemic accountability, as engineers must legally sign off on safety-critical projects where errors have life-altering environmental and public health consequences, necessitating human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to an agent.

rows.title: Biochemists and biophysicists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on knowledge work, data analysis, and complex modeling, all of which are being revolutionized by AI (e.g., AlphaFold for protein structure prediction). While physical laboratory work and team management provide a buffer, a significant portion of the role involves literature review, grant writing, and data interpretation—tasks where AI can exponentially increase productivity or automate core analytical functions.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly handle data analysis and modeling, the core of this role involves physical experimentation in complex, non-deterministic biological systems where the 'ground truth' is established through wet-lab manipulation. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-altering consequences of pharmaceutical and medical research, where human oversight is required for ethical, safety, and regulatory validation of novel scientific conclusions.

rows.title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, manual repair, and hands-on craftsmanship that AI cannot replicate. However, AI and automation are significantly impacting the design and appraisal phases through generative CAD tools and automated gemstone grading, contributing to a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and appraisal aspects are increasingly digital and automatable via CAD/CAM and AI-driven grading, the physical manipulation of precious materials requires high-dexterity interaction with a controlled workbench environment. The role retains a moderate human necessity component due to the luxury market's demand for 'hand-crafted' provenance and the trust required for high-value appraisals, though the core production tasks are highly susceptible to robotic and additive manufacturing displacement.

rows.title: Landscape architects
rows.old_rationale: Landscape architecture is a predominantly digital knowledge-based occupation where core tasks like CADD modeling, site analysis, and cost estimation are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires physical site visits and interpersonal client management, the generative design capabilities of AI can significantly accelerate the creative and technical drafting phases, potentially reducing the total human hours required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While landscape architects produce digital CADD/GIS models that are highly automatable, the final output must integrate with unpredictable, site-specific physical variables like soil composition, drainage, and local ecology. The role requires high-level synthesis of aesthetic, environmental, and regulatory constraints, and carries significant legal liability for public safety and environmental compliance that necessitates human professional oversight.

rows.title: Court reporters and simultaneous captioners
rows.old_rationale: The core product of this occupation is a verbatim digital transcript, a task where AI speech-to-text and natural language processing are already highly proficient. While legal requirements for human oversight and the need to clarify inaudible speech provide a temporary buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in transcription and real-time captioning creates extreme pressure for automation and massive productivity gains that reduce the need for human workers.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is a digital transcript, which is highly susceptible to automation via advanced speech-to-text and natural language processing agents. While the work environment is relatively controlled, the high systemic accountability stems from the legal requirement for an 'official record,' which currently necessitates a human to certify accuracy and maintain chain-of-custody for legal evidence.

rows.title: Emergency management directors
rows.old_rationale: The role involves significant digital knowledge work, such as analyzing hazards, drafting complex response plans, and managing federal funding applications, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the job requires high-stakes real-time decision-making, physical presence at command centers during disasters, and intense interpersonal coordination with government officials and the public, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and planning, the role requires high-stakes, real-time leadership in volatile, unstructured disaster environments where human judgment and moral authority are essential. The systemic accountability for life-altering decisions—such as ordering evacuations or managing public safety—cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent, as the core value lies in human-to-human coordination and ethical responsibility.

rows.title: Mining and geological engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mining and geological engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including mine design, technical reporting, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role also requires substantial physical presence for site evaluations, safety inspections, and supervising construction in remote, unpredictable environments, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can perform complex geological modeling and mine design, the output is constrained by the physical, highly volatile, and unpredictable nature of subterranean environments where real-time, site-specific adjustments are required. The role carries extreme systemic accountability for life-safety and environmental stability, necessitating human oversight and legal liability that cannot be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Hand laborers and material movers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical, involving the manual movement, cleaning, and packing of heavy materials in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI can optimize logistics, routing, and inventory tracking, the physical labor itself is resistant to AI and requires advanced robotics rather than software-based intelligence to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—moving, lifting, and cleaning—which remains highly resistant to digital execution despite advancements in robotics. While the environments are often physically demanding and unstructured, the cognitive requirements are minimal, and the role lacks the high-stakes decision-making or human-centric necessity that would protect it from automation if physical robotics were to reach parity.

rows.title: Customer service representatives
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—answering questions, processing orders, and resolving complaints—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. Large Language Models and AI agents are already capable of handling complex natural language interactions, and the BLS specifically projects a decline in employment due to automation in this sector.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by customer service representatives—processing orders, retrieving account data, and answering FAQs—are digital artifacts that can be fully executed by AI agents. While human interaction is sometimes preferred for de-escalation, the core functional output is highly automatable, and the work environment is almost exclusively controlled, digital, or structured retail settings.

rows.title: Cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, sensory judgment (taste and smell), and real-time adaptation in a high-heat, unpredictable kitchen environment. While AI-driven automation is already impacting fast food and routine institutional cooking, high-end restaurant and private household cooking remain protected by the need for complex manual skills and human creativity.
rows.new_rationale: While cooking involves following recipes, the final output is a physical transformation of matter that requires high-dexterity manipulation in a dynamic, often hazardous, and unstructured physical environment. AI can automate routine preparation, but the physical variability of ingredients and the real-time, high-stakes coordination required in a busy kitchen limit the agentic potential for full-scale autonomous replacement.

rows.title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is physical and occurs in a complex real-world environment, it is a primary target for autonomous vehicle AI, which could eventually automate long-haul highway segments. However, the job still requires significant physical presence for non-driving tasks like securing cargo, conducting inspections, and navigating 'last-mile' urban environments that remain challenging for current AI.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of the job is the physical movement of goods, which is highly susceptible to automation via autonomous vehicle technology, leading to a high agentic output potential. However, the job remains physically demanding and occurs in highly unpredictable, unstructured environments (public roads, weather, traffic) that require complex real-time navigation and safety management, while the legal and ethical liability for operating heavy machinery in public spaces keeps systemic accountability high.

rows.title: Assemblers and fabricators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation, manual dexterity, and real-time adjustments in a physical environment, which provides a significant buffer against AI. While AI and robotics are increasing efficiency and automating some repetitive tasks, a substantial portion of the work—especially in complex assembly, prototyping, and custom fabrication—still requires human presence and tactile skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is physical assembly, which remains difficult for autonomous agents to execute outside of highly controlled, repetitive factory settings. While cognitive tasks like reading schematics are increasingly automatable, the physical manipulation of components in varied or non-standardized environments requires a level of dexterity and tactile feedback that currently limits agentic replacement.

rows.title: Construction laborers and helpers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor, tool operation, and site preparation in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or specialized robotic equipment control, the vast majority of the work requires human presence, physical strength, and real-time adaptation to physical obstacles.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, volatile environments that require real-time sensory-motor adaptation, which is currently beyond the reach of autonomous agents. Because the output is a physical modification of the built environment rather than a digital artifact, the agentic potential remains extremely low despite the role's routine nature.

rows.title: General maintenance and repair workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnosing equipment issues via computer vision or managing supply orders, but the primary work of repairing buildings and machinery remains a hands-on human requirement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical intervention in unstructured, legacy environments (e.g., cramped crawlspaces, aging infrastructure) that require tactile dexterity and real-time physical problem-solving, which remains highly resistant to current agentic automation. While the cognitive tasks are routine, the systemic accountability for safety and building integrity in physical spaces necessitates human oversight, as the 'output' is a physical repair rather than a digital artifact.

rows.title: Bookkeeping, accounting, and auditing clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and involves routine information processing, data entry, and mathematical verification—tasks that AI and automated software already perform with high accuracy. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to technological change, and the shift toward 'analytical and advisory' roles is increasingly being encroached upon by advanced LLMs and specialized financial AI agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of this role—data entry, reconciliation, and report generation—are entirely digital and already highly automated by existing software, making them prime candidates for full agentic execution. Because the work occurs in a highly structured, rule-bound digital environment with minimal physical variables, there is little requirement for human presence or complex, non-routine synthesis beyond basic error checking.

rows.title: Accountants and auditors
rows.old_rationale: Accountancy is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on data analysis, regulatory compliance, and reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI and robotic process automation. While high-level advisory roles and complex forensic audits require human judgment and relationship management, the core tasks of data organization, tax computation, and anomaly detection are increasingly being automated, significantly increasing worker productivity and restructuring the entry-level labor market.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of accounting—financial statements, tax filings, and audit reports—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis, the primary barrier to full automation is not the complexity of the task, but the rigid requirement for human legal liability (CPA signatures) and the necessity of human trust in high-stakes financial and forensic contexts.

rows.title: Teacher assistants
rows.old_rationale: Teacher assistants have moderate exposure because AI can significantly automate their administrative and instructional support tasks, such as grading, lesson preparation, and providing personalized tutoring through AI agents. However, a large portion of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and assisting students with special needs or personal hygiene, which require a physical human presence that AI cannot replace.
rows.new_rationale: While recordkeeping and instructional reinforcement have high digital potential, the core of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and direct care for students with disabilities, which occur in highly unpredictable, unstructured environments. The necessity for human presence is paramount due to the requirement for emotional labor, physical safety, and the ethical responsibility of supervising minors, which cannot be delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and digital monitoring. While AI-powered computer vision is rapidly automating the 'surveillance' and 'monitoring' aspects of the job, the core requirements of physical patrolling, detaining suspects, and responding to real-world emergencies provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of monitoring and surveillance is highly susceptible to AI automation, as the output is primarily digital detection and reporting. While the physical patrol and de-escalation aspects require human presence in unpredictable environments, the increasing reliance on AI-integrated cameras suggests that the 'agentic' portion of the job—identifying and flagging threats—is rapidly shifting to software, leaving only the high-stakes, liability-heavy physical intervention to humans.

rows.title: Carpenters
rows.old_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, material estimation, or business management, the core work of cutting, fitting, and installing materials remains resistant to automation.
rows.new_rationale: Carpentry is fundamentally a physical trade where the output is a structural modification of the real world, making it highly resistant to digital agentic execution. The work environment is extremely unstructured, involving variable site conditions, physical hazards, and the need for real-time, on-site problem solving that requires human dexterity and physical presence.

rows.title: Market research analysts
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data collection, statistical analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with AI's strengths in processing large datasets and generating insights. While human judgment is still needed for high-level strategy and client relationships, AI can now automate the vast majority of the technical and administrative tasks that define the daily work of an analyst.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—data analysis, forecasting, and report generation—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of processing big data. While the work requires synthesis of complex market variables, it occurs in a highly controlled, office-based environment with minimal physical unpredictability, and the human element is largely limited to stakeholder communication rather than essential human-to-human service.

rows.title: Computer support specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of diagnosing technical issues, documenting problems, and guiding users through digital workflows are highly susceptible to AI automation via advanced chatbots and LLM-driven troubleshooting. While some physical hardware setup and complex onsite networking remain, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that AI can increasingly handle, as evidenced by the BLS's projected decline in employment due to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by computer support specialists involve digital diagnostics and software-based remediation, which are highly susceptible to automation by AI agents. While some physical hardware repair exists, the core value is increasingly informational and remote, making the role highly vulnerable to displacement by autonomous systems that can execute fixes directly within the network environment.

rows.title: Financial managers
rows.old_rationale: Financial management is a predominantly digital knowledge-work occupation focused on data analysis, reporting, and forecasting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires significant human judgment, strategic advisory, and leadership, the core technical tasks of monitoring financial details and preparing statements are being rapidly transformed by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Financial management is highly susceptible to automation because the primary outputs—reports, forecasts, and investment commands—are digital artifacts that can be executed directly by software agents. While the role requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex market variables, the work occurs in a highly controlled, digital environment, and the primary barrier to full automation is the high-stakes systemic accountability and legal liability that requires a human to sign off on fiduciary and regulatory decisions.

rows.title: Material moving machine operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is physical operation of heavy machinery in dynamic environments like warehouses and construction sites, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI-driven computer vision and autonomous navigation are increasingly being integrated into forklifts and conveyor systems, leading to moderate exposure as these technologies augment or replace human operators in structured settings.
rows.new_rationale: While the core task is physical movement, the high-stakes nature of operating heavy machinery in dynamic, unstructured environments like construction sites or ports creates significant physical and safety constraints that are difficult for current AI to navigate autonomously. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the extreme legal and life-safety liability inherent in operating heavy equipment, where a machine error can result in catastrophic physical harm.

rows.title: Physicians and surgeons
rows.old_rationale: Physicians and surgeons have moderate exposure because while AI can significantly automate digital tasks like diagnostic image interpretation (radiology), medical history analysis, and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical presence, manual dexterity for procedures, and complex interpersonal communication. The high-stakes nature of medical liability and the need for human empathy in patient care provide a natural barrier to full automation, though AI will drastically increase productivity in information-heavy specialties.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics and data synthesis, the final output of this role is a physical intervention on a biological system, which remains highly constrained by the unpredictability of human physiology and the need for real-time, high-stakes physical dexterity. Furthermore, the necessity for human empathy in patient care and the absolute requirement for legal and ethical accountability for life-altering decisions make this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Police and detectives
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical presence, emergency response, and manual tasks like making arrests and patrolling, which are resistant to AI. However, a significant portion of the work involves digital tasks such as writing detailed reports, searching databases, and analyzing evidence, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and report generation, the core value of this occupation lies in physical intervention, de-escalation, and the exercise of state-sanctioned force in highly volatile, unstructured environments. The requirement for human moral authority, legal liability for life-altering decisions, and the necessity of physical presence in unpredictable, high-stakes scenarios makes this role largely resistant to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Electricians
rows.old_rationale: The core work of an electrician is highly physical, involving manual installation, repair, and navigation of complex, unpredictable physical environments like construction sites and crawl spaces. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, diagnostic calculations, or scheduling, it cannot perform the tactile labor or real-time physical problem-solving required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: While electricians perform complex diagnostic reasoning, their output is fundamentally physical, requiring navigation of highly unstructured, legacy, and hazardous environments that remain beyond the reach of current autonomous robotics. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the life-safety implications of electrical work, where code compliance and physical liability necessitate human oversight that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Social workers
rows.old_rationale: Social work involves a high degree of interpersonal interaction, empathy, and crisis management in physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against full automation. However, AI will significantly impact the profession by automating administrative tasks like case documentation, assisting in resource research, and providing diagnostic support tools for clinical practitioners.
rows.new_rationale: While social workers manage significant data and documentation, their core value is derived from high-stakes, real-time human intervention in volatile, unstructured environments like crisis response and home visits. The necessity of human empathy, moral judgment, and legal accountability for life-altering decisions makes the role fundamentally resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Computer and information systems managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving strategic planning, analysis, and technical oversight that AI can significantly augment. While high-level leadership, vendor negotiation, and personnel management provide a buffer of human judgment, the core tasks of assessing IT needs, monitoring security, and directing technical workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly susceptible to AI because its primary output—strategic IT planning, system architecture, and resource allocation—is entirely digital and increasingly executable by autonomous agents. While the job requires high-level cognitive synthesis, the environment is a controlled, predictable office setting, and the core value relies more on technical authority and accountability for system-critical failures than on irreplaceable human-to-human emotional bonds.

rows.title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical interaction in a highly unpredictable environment (human hair and skin). While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like appointment scheduling, marketing, or visualizing hairstyles for clients, the physical act of cutting, coloring, and styling hair remains a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this profession is rooted in physical, high-touch service and the human-to-human social interaction inherent in the salon experience, which is difficult to replicate via digital agents. While the cognitive tasks of styling can be modeled, the physical execution requires navigating the highly variable, non-standardized geometry of human anatomy in a real-time, unstructured environment, making full automation extremely unlikely.

rows.title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing price proposals, monitoring inventory, and reviewing financial reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While high-stakes negotiations and physical site visits provide a buffer, the majority of the work is digital information processing that can be significantly streamlined by AI, leading to higher productivity per worker and a restructuring of the procurement process.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—contracts, purchase orders, and supply chain logistics—is inherently digital and highly susceptible to automation via AI agents that can monitor inventory and execute transactions. While the role requires negotiation and relationship management, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the primary value is transactional efficiency rather than a unique human-to-human bond.

rows.title: Special education teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the digital and administrative aspects of the role, such as drafting Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), adapting lesson plans, and tracking student data. However, the core of the job requires intense physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time behavioral management for students with complex disabilities, which AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with IEP documentation and lesson planning, the core value of the role is rooted in the physical, empathetic, and real-time human interaction required to support students with diverse disabilities. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the behavioral and emotional variables of students, and the legal and ethical accountability for student development and safety remains strictly non-delegatable.

rows.title: Preschool teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical care, emotional support, and real-time social interaction with children under age five, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, curriculum design, and progress reporting, the primary job requires physical presence and human empathy to manage safety and developmental needs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in the human-to-human bond and the physical, real-time management of young children, which is highly resistant to digital automation. While curriculum planning has cognitive complexity, the execution requires navigating the volatile, unstructured physical environment of a classroom and maintaining a high level of ethical and safety-related accountability that cannot be delegated to an agent.

rows.title: Construction managers
rows.old_rationale: Construction management is a hybrid role that combines digital knowledge work—such as cost estimation, scheduling, and contract analysis—with essential physical presence. While AI can significantly automate budgeting and logistical planning, the core of the job requires real-world site inspections, physical problem-solving, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with tradesworkers and inspectors that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: While construction managers use digital tools for scheduling and budgeting, their primary output is the physical coordination of complex, high-stakes construction sites, which remains largely resistant to full automation. The job requires navigating highly unpredictable physical environments and managing human-centric negotiations, safety compliance, and legal liability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Bus drivers
rows.old_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is a direct long-term threat to the core task of vehicle operation, the role involves significant physical and interpersonal responsibilities that AI cannot yet replicate. School bus drivers must manage student behavior and safety, while transit and charter drivers assist passengers with disabilities and handle physical luggage, creating a substantial barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of the job—transporting passengers from point A to point B—is highly susceptible to automation via autonomous vehicle technology, which is why the agentic potential is high. However, the role remains physically constrained by the high-stakes, unpredictable nature of navigating public roads and the critical, non-delegatable legal and ethical responsibility for passenger safety, particularly when managing children or passengers with disabilities.

rows.title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
