rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this role is almost entirely digital, consisting of financial transactions and advisory artifacts that are already being executed by software at scale. While the work involves high-level synthesis of market data and complex deal structuring, the primary barrier to full automation is the requirement for human-to-human trust in high-stakes advisory relationships and the non-delegatable legal/ethical liability inherent in managing client assets and corporate mergers.

rows.title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments like crawl spaces and construction sites. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like cost estimation, blueprint analysis via BIM, and scheduling, it cannot perform the essential hands-on installation and repair work.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in highly unstructured, legacy environments (crawlspaces, walls, industrial plants) where AI agents lack the mobility and tactile dexterity to operate. While planning and diagnostics involve complex synthesis, the final output is a physical repair that carries high systemic liability regarding safety, building codes, and property damage, making full delegation to autonomous agents currently impossible.

rows.title: Postal service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as delivering parcels and navigating outdoor environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI and advanced automation are significantly impacting the sorting and processing components of the job, while the shift toward digital communication reduces overall mail volume and labor demand.
rows.new_rationale: While sorting and processing are highly automatable, the 'last mile' delivery remains a physical task requiring navigation of unstructured, real-world environments (weather, traffic, varied terrain). The job has low cognitive synthesis requirements and minimal ontological necessity, as the core value is the physical movement of goods rather than human-to-human connection.

rows.title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate significant portions of the job—such as drafting leases, financial reporting, budgeting, and coordinating maintenance requests—the role still requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution. The exposure is high because the administrative and analytical 'office' tasks that comprise a large part of the day are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While financial and administrative tasks are highly digitizable, the role requires navigating unpredictable physical environments and complex human interpersonal dynamics, such as mediating disputes and enforcing rules. The necessity for human empathy, moral authority in conflict resolution, and legal accountability for property safety and fair housing compliance creates a significant barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, physical stamina, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable environments like construction sites or repair shops. While AI and robotics can automate routine assembly-line welding, the specialized, non-routine tasks and the need for physical presence in varied locations provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.
rows.new_rationale: While the cognitive tasks of blueprint reading and heat adjustment are increasingly automatable, the physical output requires high-dexterity manipulation in unstructured, often hazardous environments that are difficult for current robotics to navigate. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of structural welds, where failure can lead to catastrophic infrastructure collapse, necessitating human oversight for safety and liability.

rows.title: Training and development specialists
rows.old_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—curriculum design, instructional modules, and training delivery—is increasingly digital and can be fully automated or executed by AI agents. While the role requires interpersonal facilitation, the physical environment is highly controlled (offices or virtual platforms), and the primary value is informational rather than requiring a unique human physical presence or high-stakes legal liability.

rows.title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is highly susceptible to automation through autonomous vehicle technology (AI), the job currently requires significant physical presence for tasks like loading luggage, assisting passengers with disabilities, and performing vehicle maintenance. The transition to fully driverless fleets faces regulatory and technical hurdles in complex environments, but the long-term trajectory for this digital-adjacent physical task is one of high potential displacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation—transporting a passenger from point A to point B—is highly susceptible to automation via autonomous vehicle software, which effectively replaces the driver's primary output. While the physical environment (traffic, weather, road hazards) remains volatile and unpredictable, the legal and ethical liability for passenger safety remains a critical, non-delegatable burden that currently acts as the primary barrier to full AI replacement.

rows.title: Financial analysts
rows.old_rationale: Financial analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the processing of large datasets, trend analysis, and the generation of reports—all areas where AI excels. While high-level strategy and client relationships provide some insulation, the core tasks of data evaluation, risk assessment, and financial modeling are being rapidly automated or significantly enhanced by AI, leading to major restructuring of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: Financial analysis is highly susceptible to automation because the core output—investment recommendations, portfolio adjustments, and risk reports—is entirely digital and can be executed directly by software agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis of complex market data, the environment is highly structured (digital markets), and the primary human necessity remains centered on trust and legal liability rather than physical or emotional interaction.

rows.title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnostic software, scheduling, or energy load calculations, it cannot perform the physical installation, repair, or handling of hazardous materials required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this job involves physical manipulation of hardware in highly unstructured, cramped, and volatile environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current or near-future autonomous agents. While diagnostic software can assist with cognitive synthesis, the final execution requires physical dexterity, navigation of unpredictable site conditions, and significant legal/safety accountability for handling hazardous pressurized gases and electrical systems.

rows.title: Compliance officers
rows.old_rationale: Compliance work is primarily digital and involves analyzing vast amounts of regulatory text, auditing data, and generating reports—all areas where LLMs and automated systems excel. While high-level judgment and interpersonal investigations provide some insulation, the core tasks of monitoring, risk assessment, and documentation are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—reports, policy implementation, and risk assessments—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of monitoring regulatory databases. While the work requires high-level synthesis of complex legal frameworks, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the primary barrier to full automation is the requirement for human legal and ethical accountability in high-stakes compliance decisions.

rows.title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are physical and interpersonal, requiring real-time human presence to maintain security, conduct searches, and physically restrain individuals. AI exposure is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like report writing and log entry, or enhancing surveillance systems, but it cannot replace the physical oversight and intervention required in correctional and courtroom environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves physical intervention, de-escalation, and the use of force in highly volatile, unstructured environments where AI lacks the physical agency to intervene safely. Furthermore, the role requires non-delegatable moral and legal authority over human liberty, meaning the 'human-in-the-loop' is a fundamental requirement for both ethical and liability reasons.

rows.title: School and career counselors and advisors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-touch interpersonal work and digital information processing. AI can significantly automate the 'hard' data tasks like analyzing student performance trends, matching aptitudes to career paths, and drafting résumés, but the core functions of emotional support, crisis intervention (reporting abuse), and navigating complex human relationships in a school setting provide a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize data to provide career paths or academic plans, the core value of this role lies in the human-to-human therapeutic bond, empathy, and the moral authority required to handle sensitive issues like abuse or behavioral crises. The systemic accountability is high because the counselor bears the ethical and legal responsibility for the well-being of vulnerable students, a duty that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—legal research, document drafting, and information organization—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models. While some interpersonal and courtroom logistics remain, AI can already automate or significantly accelerate the vast majority of a paralegal's workload, leading to the BLS's projection of 0% job growth despite high demand for legal services.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of a paralegal—legal research, document drafting, and evidence organization—are highly digital and increasingly automatable by AI agents, leading to a high agentic output potential. While the work requires significant cognitive synthesis to navigate complex legal frameworks, it occurs in a highly controlled office environment, and the ultimate systemic accountability and legal liability remain with the attorney, limiting the necessity for a human in the paralegal role itself.

rows.title: Fitness trainers and instructors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation requires physical presence to demonstrate exercises, correct form in real-time to prevent injury, and provide high-touch motivational support. While AI can automate peripheral tasks like workout programming, nutrition planning, and social media marketing, it cannot replace the physical safety monitoring and interpersonal accountability that define the role.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate fitness plans and track metrics, the core value of this role is the human-to-human motivational bond and the physical, real-time correction of movement to prevent injury. The necessity for a human presence to provide empathy, accountability, and immediate physical safety oversight makes this role highly resistant to full automation, despite the digital nature of the planning component.

rows.title: Food service managers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like staff scheduling, inventory ordering, and payroll management, the core of the job requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Managers must physically inspect facilities for safety, handle unpredictable interpersonal conflicts with staff and customers, and perform manual labor during peak hours, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and payroll are highly automatable, the core of the role involves navigating a volatile, high-stakes physical environment (kitchens, dining rooms) where real-time human intervention is required for safety and service recovery. The job's value is heavily tied to human-to-human conflict resolution and the physical oversight of a dynamic, unpredictable workspace that remains difficult for autonomous agents to navigate effectively.

rows.title: Industrial engineers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial engineering is heavily focused on data analysis, systems design, and process optimization—tasks where AI and machine learning excel. While the role requires physical site visits and human collaboration to understand workflows, the core work product is digital analysis and modeling, making the occupation highly susceptible to significant productivity gains and restructuring through AI-driven automation.
rows.new_rationale: Industrial engineers produce high-level system designs and process optimizations that are increasingly digital and executable by AI-driven automation, leading to high agentic potential. However, their work requires navigating complex, unstructured physical environments (factories, hospitals) and carries significant systemic accountability for safety and operational integrity, which necessitates human oversight and ethical judgment.

rows.title: Firefighters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of firefighting are intensely physical and require real-time presence in unpredictable, hazardous environments where AI cannot currently operate. While AI may enhance peripheral tasks like incident reporting, equipment maintenance scheduling, and predictive wildfire modeling, the essential work of rescuing victims and extinguishing fires remains a human-centric manual task.
rows.new_rationale: Firefighting is defined by high-stakes physical intervention in volatile, unstructured environments where digital output is irrelevant to the core task of life-saving. The requirement for real-time, ethical decision-making in life-or-death scenarios creates a non-delegatable human necessity and accountability that cannot be offloaded to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Pharmacists
rows.old_rationale: Pharmacists face moderate exposure because their core knowledge-based tasks—checking drug interactions, verifying dosages, and analyzing patient records—are highly susceptible to AI automation and digital optimization. However, the role remains anchored by physical requirements such as administering immunizations, performing clinical rounds, and providing real-time, empathetic patient counseling that requires human trust and physical presence.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical act of dispensing medication is increasingly automated, the pharmacist's core value lies in high-stakes clinical judgment, drug interaction synthesis, and the irreplaceable human element of patient counseling and empathy. The role carries immense legal and ethical liability for life-altering errors, ensuring that while AI can assist in data processing, the final accountability and human-to-human trust remain strictly non-delegatable.

rows.title: Recreation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual instruction, and real-time interpersonal interaction in unpredictable environments like camps and community centers. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, program planning, and creating newsletters, it cannot replace the physical supervision, safety monitoring, and emotional leadership required to manage group activities.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human interaction, mentorship, and physical presence, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While administrative tasks are automatable, the primary work involves navigating unpredictable physical environments and managing group dynamics, requiring a level of human empathy and real-time physical judgment that remains outside the scope of current autonomous systems.

rows.title: Diesel service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in a hands-on environment. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software and technical manuals used by technicians, it cannot perform the physical repairs, parts replacement, or tool manipulation that define the job.
rows.new_rationale: While diagnostic software can identify issues, the final output requires complex physical manipulation of heavy, greasy, and often seized mechanical components in unstructured, non-standardized environments. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the life-critical nature of vehicle safety, where human judgment remains the final arbiter of whether a repair is sufficient for road-worthiness.

rows.title: Loan officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of loan officers—analyzing financial data, verifying documentation, and assessing risk—are fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. While complex commercial loans and relationship-based mortgage sales still require human judgment and interpersonal skills, the rapid advancement of automated underwriting and AI-driven customer service significantly reduces the need for human intervention in routine loan processing.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a loan officer is a digital decision (approval/denial) based on data, which is already heavily automated by underwriting software, giving it high agentic potential. While complex commercial loans require human judgment, the environment is highly structured and digital, meaning the primary human necessity is limited to client relationship management and final legal sign-off rather than physical or unpredictable labor.

rows.title: Mechanical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mechanical engineering is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD, simulations, and data analysis, all of which are being rapidly enhanced by AI to automate complex design iterations and predictive modeling. While the role requires physical prototyping, site visits, and hardware testing, the core intellectual work of designing and analyzing mechanical systems is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While mechanical engineers produce highly complex digital artifacts (CAD models, simulations, and specifications) that AI can increasingly generate, the final physical implementation requires navigating unpredictable, real-world material constraints and failure modes. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the legal and ethical necessity for a licensed human to sign off on designs where failure could result in catastrophic physical harm, ensuring that the 'buck' remains with a human professional.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing circuits, modeling systems, and writing documentation is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While physical testing, site visits, and manufacturing supervision provide a buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in CAD, simulation, and coding significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the knowledge-work components.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and simulation work is increasingly digital and agentic, the role requires high-level synthesis of complex, non-routine physical systems. The necessity for human oversight is driven primarily by systemic accountability—the legal and ethical liability for safety-critical infrastructure—rather than a requirement for human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Radiologic and MRI technologists
rows.old_rationale: While AI is revolutionizing image analysis and quality control, the core of this role requires physical presence to position patients, administer contrast agents, and operate heavy machinery in a clinical setting. AI will significantly enhance productivity and image evaluation, but the physical and interpersonal requirements of patient care provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the operation of imaging equipment is increasingly automated, the role remains tethered to the physical, unpredictable task of positioning patients who may be in pain, immobile, or distressed. The human element is essential for patient safety, emotional regulation, and the physical manipulation required in a clinical environment, maintaining a high barrier to full agentic replacement despite the digital nature of the final diagnostic image.

rows.title: Telecommunications technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical labor, including climbing towers, installing hardware, and manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can assist with remote diagnostics, network optimization, and predictive maintenance, the physical necessity of splicing cables and mounting equipment ensures the role remains primarily human-driven.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic and monitoring aspects of the job are increasingly digital, the core value remains physical installation and repair in highly unstructured, hazardous environments like towers, crawlspaces, and rooftops. The requirement for physical dexterity, navigation of unpredictable site conditions, and the high-stakes nature of critical infrastructure maintenance creates a significant barrier to full automation, despite the digital nature of the signals being managed.

rows.title: Graphic designers
rows.old_rationale: Graphic design is a fundamentally digital occupation where core tasks—image generation, layout creation, and photo editing—are being rapidly transformed by generative AI. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, the automation of production tasks significantly increases individual productivity, likely reducing the total number of designers needed for routine commercial work.
rows.new_rationale: Graphic design output is almost entirely digital, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven generation and automated layout execution. While the role requires creative synthesis and client communication, the core tasks occur in highly controlled digital environments, leaving little room for physical or high-stakes systemic accountability that would necessitate a human agent.

rows.title: Data scientists
rows.old_rationale: Data science is a fully digital occupation centered on coding, statistical modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity or superiority. While human judgment is still needed for business context and ethical oversight, AI can now automate significant portions of the data pipeline, including cleaning raw data, generating complex code, and even suggesting optimal model architectures.
rows.new_rationale: Data science is highly susceptible to automation because the entire workflow—from data cleaning to algorithmic model deployment—is digital and executable by software agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis, the work occurs in a controlled, virtual environment, and the human necessity is largely limited to stakeholder communication and final strategic sign-off rather than physical or empathetic presence.

rows.title: Logisticians
rows.old_rationale: Logisticians perform high-level knowledge work that is primarily digital, involving data analysis, forecasting, and supply chain optimization—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires human-to-human negotiation and real-world problem-solving for physical disruptions, the core functions of inventory management and route optimization are increasingly being automated or enhanced by sophisticated AI algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: Logistics is fundamentally a data-driven orchestration of digital systems, making it highly susceptible to autonomous agents that can optimize supply chains and execute procurement commands in real-time. While the work involves managing physical goods, the logistician's role is primarily to manipulate the digital representation of those goods, meaning the 'final output' is almost entirely software-executable.

rows.title: Human resources managers
rows.old_rationale: Human resources management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation where AI can significantly automate recruitment screening, payroll processing, and regulatory compliance reporting. While the role requires high-level interpersonal skills for dispute mediation and strategic leadership, the vast majority of the administrative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and analytical output of HR management is highly digitizable and executable by agents, the core value remains deeply rooted in human-to-human mediation, trust, and ethical judgment. The role requires navigating complex, high-stakes organizational dynamics where the human element is not just a preference but a functional necessity for conflict resolution and legal liability.

rows.title: Dental hygienists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision manual dexterity and physical procedures performed on patients in a clinical setting, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like analyzing X-rays or automating patient documentation, the primary work of cleaning teeth and conducting physical exams remains insulated from automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic and educational aspects of the role involve cognitive synthesis, the core value is derived from high-precision, physical intervention within the highly variable and sensitive environment of a human oral cavity. The necessity for human presence is driven by the need to manage patient anxiety and physical comfort, while the systemic accountability remains high due to the invasive nature of the work and the potential for life-altering health consequences.

rows.title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as logistics planning, budgeting, and supply chain optimization, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains. However, the core job also requires physical presence for facility inspections, safety compliance monitoring, and high-stakes interpersonal leadership in unpredictable environments like warehouses and railyards, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of this role—logistics optimization, inventory tracking, and supply chain coordination—are increasingly digital and highly amenable to autonomous agent execution. While the role involves physical environments like warehouses, the manager's primary output is the strategic orchestration of digital systems, though they retain significant systemic accountability for safety compliance and high-stakes operational disruptions.

rows.title: Tutors
rows.old_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of tutoring—instruction, feedback, and lesson planning—is highly digitizable and can be fully executed by AI agents, leading to high agentic potential. While human presence remains valuable for motivation and emotional support, the technical delivery of academic content is increasingly decoupled from the necessity of a human agent.

rows.title: Woodworkers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of materials, manual dexterity, and real-time sensory monitoring in a workshop environment, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize digital design (CAD) and CNC programming, the physical assembly, finishing, and machine maintenance require a human presence that limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While CNC automation handles much of the precision manufacturing, the physical nature of wood—a non-uniform, organic material—requires constant human intervention to manage variability, grain defects, and physical assembly in unstructured shop environments. The output is fundamentally a physical object, limiting the agentic potential of software to the digital design phase rather than the final, tactile execution.

rows.title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision physical labor, tactile sensitivity, and real-time human interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral business tasks like scheduling or inventory management, the primary service of cleaning, shaping, and decorating nails remains a manual task that is difficult to automate with current robotics.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in physical, tactile service and the human-to-human social interaction inherent in personal care, which makes it highly resistant to digital agentic replacement. While the physical environment is relatively controlled, the requirement for precise, safe manipulation of sharp tools on variable human anatomy necessitates a human presence that software cannot currently replicate.

rows.title: Chefs and head cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is highly physical and sensory, requiring manual dexterity, real-time human leadership in a fast-paced kitchen, and the use of taste and smell. While AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks like menu planning, inventory management, and recipe generation, it cannot replace the physical oversight and hands-on food preparation essential to the role.
rows.new_rationale: While chefs perform complex cognitive synthesis in menu design and kitchen management, the final output is a physical, perishable product that requires real-time manipulation of volatile biological materials in a high-pressure, unstructured physical environment. The role retains significant human necessity due to the sensory-based quality control and the leadership required to manage a human team, making full automation of the 'chef' role—as opposed to mere food preparation—highly improbable.

rows.title: Medical records specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the processing, classification, and entry of data into electronic systems. AI is already highly capable of natural language processing and automated medical coding, which directly automates the core tasks of reviewing records and assigning clinical codes, significantly reducing the need for human intervention.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of medical records specialists—data entry, classification, and coding—are entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents that can parse clinical documentation and assign standardized codes. While the role requires adherence to privacy laws and accuracy, the work is performed in a controlled, sedentary digital environment, leaving little room for physical or human-centric variables that would resist algorithmic replacement.

rows.title: Automotive body and glass repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks are highly physical, involving manual dexterity, welding, and the use of pneumatic tools in unpredictable repair environments that are difficult to automate. AI's primary impact is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like cost estimation and damage assessment via computer vision, but the physical restoration of vehicles remains a human-centric trade.
rows.new_rationale: The work is fundamentally physical, requiring high-dexterity manipulation of materials in unstructured, often cramped environments that are difficult for current robotics to navigate. While the cognitive tasks of diagnosis are increasingly automatable, the systemic accountability for structural integrity and safety in vehicle repair remains a high-stakes, non-delegatable human responsibility.

rows.title: Drafters
rows.old_rationale: Drafters perform work that is almost entirely digital, converting conceptual sketches into technical CAD and BIM models. AI is rapidly advancing in generative design and automated technical drawing, allowing for the immediate translation of engineering specifications into precise models, which significantly reduces the human labor required for these tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a drafter is a digital CAD/BIM file, which is highly susceptible to automation as AI agents can increasingly interpret specifications and generate precise technical schematics. While the work requires technical accuracy and adherence to standards, the environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, leaving systemic accountability as the primary barrier to full automation, as a human must still sign off on the safety and legal compliance of the final design.

rows.title: Speech-language pathologists
rows.old_rationale: Speech-language pathology involves a significant amount of digital-adjacent work, such as analyzing speech patterns, documenting progress, and developing treatment plans, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for swallowing therapy, real-time interpersonal coaching, and high-stakes clinical judgment that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostic data analysis and generating therapy exercises, the core value of the role lies in the human-to-human therapeutic alliance, emotional support, and the physical, real-time adjustment of techniques based on a patient's immediate physiological and psychological response. The high systemic accountability stems from the critical nature of swallowing and communication therapy, where errors can lead to life-threatening medical complications, necessitating human professional liability.

rows.title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, feeding, and physically restraining patients, which AI cannot perform. While AI can assist with monitoring patient behavior and automating documentation or reporting, the high requirement for real-time human presence, physical stamina, and interpersonal compassion creates a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in physical presence, patient safety, and the human-to-human bond, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the volatile nature of patients in psychiatric settings, requiring real-time physical intervention and moral judgment that remains strictly human-centric.

rows.title: Massage therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of soft tissues and real-time human empathy in a physical setting. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, marketing, and documentation, it cannot replace the tactile skill and physical presence required for massage therapy.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, tactile manipulation of human tissue, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software, resulting in extremely low agentic output potential. While the work requires clinical assessment, the primary product is the human-to-human therapeutic bond and the physical presence required to safely navigate the client's unique physiological feedback in real-time.

rows.title: Producers and directors
rows.old_rationale: Producers and directors are highly exposed because their core work products—scripts, visual aesthetics, and edited video—are increasingly being generated or enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires significant human leadership and physical presence on set, the rapid advancement of generative AI in pre-production (scripting/storyboarding) and post-production (editing/VFX) allows a single director to do the work of a much larger team, potentially consolidating the industry.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (film/media) is increasingly digital and susceptible to AI-driven production workflows, the role requires high-level creative synthesis and the management of complex, unpredictable human and physical variables on set. The core value remains deeply rooted in human leadership, artistic vision, and the ability to foster authentic performances, which are difficult to fully delegate to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Roofers
rows.old_rationale: Roofing is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, balance, and heavy lifting. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like drone-based inspections or material estimation, the core work of installing and repairing shingles and membranes remains entirely dependent on human labor.
rows.new_rationale: Roofing is a highly physical, site-specific trade that requires navigating unstructured, volatile, and dangerous environments, making it extremely resistant to digital agentic replacement. While the cognitive tasks are manageable, the necessity for real-time physical interaction with non-standardized, aging structures and the high-stakes liability of structural integrity ensure that human labor remains essential.

rows.title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report writing, and regulatory cross-referencing, the core duties require physical inspections of worksites, manual testing with specialized equipment, and real-time human interaction to conduct safety trainings and emergency investigations.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can analyze data and suggest safety protocols, the core value of this role lies in physical site inspections, navigating volatile environments like construction sites or mines, and enforcing compliance in unpredictable real-world settings. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical burden of life-critical safety decisions, which cannot be fully offloaded to an autonomous agent that lacks the physical presence and moral authority to manage human risk.

rows.title: Physician assistants
rows.old_rationale: Physician assistants perform a mix of high-level knowledge work and physical clinical tasks. While AI will significantly augment diagnostic accuracy, treatment planning, and administrative documentation, the role requires physical presence for examinations, surgical procedures, and hands-on treatments like stitching wounds, as well as complex interpersonal counseling that is difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics and data synthesis, the PA role requires physical intervention (stitching, setting bones, physical exams) and high-stakes, real-time decision-making in unpredictable clinical environments. The core value is deeply rooted in human-to-human trust, empathy, and non-delegatable legal liability for life-altering medical outcomes, making full automation of the role impossible.

rows.title: Photographers
rows.old_rationale: Photography is a hybrid role where the physical act of capturing real-world events (weddings, news, portraits) provides a buffer, but the digital output is highly vulnerable to AI. Generative AI can now create high-quality commercial and stock imagery from scratch and automate complex post-production tasks, significantly reducing the billable hours and demand for professional photographers in commercial and creative sectors.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of photography is a digital file, which is increasingly susceptible to generative AI synthesis, giving this occupation a very high agentic potential. While professional photography requires artistic judgment and physical navigation of environments, the actual 'product' is largely informational and aesthetic, lacking the high-stakes systemic accountability or deep human-necessity requirements found in fields like medicine or law.

rows.title: Construction and building inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical site visits and digital knowledge work. AI and computer vision can significantly automate the 'plans examiner' and report-writing functions, while remote inspection technology is already projected to reduce labor demand. However, the core requirement for physical presence to navigate tight spaces, use manual testing equipment, and verify real-world conditions provides a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While plan reviews and report generation are increasingly digital, the core value of the role relies on physical presence in highly unstructured, hazardous environments where AI cannot yet navigate or interpret complex, non-standardized physical defects. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and public safety liability, meaning the final 'stamp of approval' requires a human agent to assume responsibility for life-critical infrastructure.

rows.title: Broadcast, sound, and video technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical labor (setting up hardware, rigging lights, running cables) and digital knowledge work (mixing sound, editing video, managing broadcast signals). While the physical installation tasks are resistant to AI, the digital components—such as sound leveling, video editing, and signal monitoring—are being rapidly automated by AI tools, leading to higher productivity and a projected decline in specialized roles like sound and broadcast technicians.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is increasingly digital, as signal processing, mixing, and editing are highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven software agents. While the job involves physical setup in varied environments—which provides some resistance to full automation—the primary value is technical execution rather than human-to-human connection or high-stakes legal liability, making it a prime candidate for displacement by integrated robotic and software systems.

rows.title: Database administrators and architects
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, involving coding (SQL), system architecture, and data management—all areas where AI is exceptionally proficient. AI can already automate routine DBA tasks like performance tuning, query optimization, and security monitoring, while LLMs are highly capable of generating and debugging the complex schemas and scripts used by database architects.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this role is entirely digital and exists within software environments, making it highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of managing infrastructure and code. While the work requires high-level architectural synthesis, it occurs in a strictly controlled, logical environment, leaving systemic accountability as the primary barrier to full automation due to the high-stakes nature of data security and system integrity.

rows.title: Butchers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-time interaction with physical products in a cold, unpredictable environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory tracking and sales forecasting, the primary duties of cutting, deboning, and handling meat are resistant to digital automation and require specialized robotics rather than AI software.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is a physical transformation of biological material, which is highly resistant to digital agentic execution despite the potential for robotic automation in controlled settings. The work environment is physically volatile, involving sharp tools and variable raw materials, requiring human dexterity and judgment to manage non-standardized physical inputs that remain difficult for autonomous systems to navigate safely.

rows.title: Phlebotomists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a highly tactile, physical procedure (venipuncture) performed on human patients in unpredictable clinical settings, which is resistant to AI automation. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data entry, sample tracking, and scheduling, the primary value of the role lies in manual dexterity and providing interpersonal emotional support to anxious patients.
rows.new_rationale: While the digital output (data entry) is minimal, the core task is a high-stakes physical intervention on a human body, which requires significant dexterity and real-time adaptation to patient physiology. The necessity for human presence is high due to the requirement for patient reassurance and the ethical/legal liability inherent in invasive medical procedures, making full automation difficult despite the routine nature of the blood draw itself.

rows.title: Respiratory therapists
rows.old_rationale: Respiratory therapy involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on patient care, such as performing chest physiotherapy, intubating patients, and maintaining equipment in real-time clinical environments. While AI will heavily assist in diagnostic analysis, ventilator setting optimization, and patient monitoring, the core of the job requires physical presence and interpersonal interaction that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in monitoring and diagnostic data analysis, the core value of the role involves high-stakes physical interventions, such as airway clearance and emergency ventilator management, which require real-time physical dexterity and adaptability in volatile clinical environments. The necessity for human empathy, bedside trust, and the non-delegatable legal liability for life-critical patient outcomes ensures that the role remains fundamentally human-centric despite the increasing integration of digital diagnostic tools.

rows.title: Writers and authors
rows.old_rationale: The core work product of writers and authors is entirely digital and text-based, which aligns perfectly with the primary capabilities of Large Language Models. AI can now generate drafts, conduct research, and perform editing tasks across various formats like blogs, copy, and scripts, significantly increasing productivity while simultaneously threatening to automate many entry-level and routine writing roles.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this profession is almost entirely digital, making it highly susceptible to AI agents that can generate, edit, and publish content directly. While high-level creative synthesis remains a human strength, the lack of physical environmental constraints and the relatively low systemic liability (compared to medicine or engineering) mean that AI can perform the vast majority of the core tasks with minimal human intervention.

rows.title: Art directors
rows.old_rationale: Art directors work in a fundamentally digital domain where AI is rapidly advancing in image generation, layout design, and video synthesis. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and client communication, the core work product can be heavily augmented or automated by AI, significantly increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of directors needed to oversee creative projects.
rows.new_rationale: Art direction is highly susceptible to AI because the final output is almost entirely digital or conceptual, allowing agents to generate and iterate on visual assets rapidly. While the role requires high-level creative synthesis and human-to-human negotiation to align client vision with artistic execution, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the accountability is primarily professional rather than life-critical.

rows.title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks that AI cannot perform, such as restraining animals, administering anesthesia, performing surgery prep, and providing emergency first aid. However, AI has significant exposure in the diagnostic and administrative aspects of the job, including analyzing lab results, interpreting X-rays, and managing patient records, which may increase individual productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The role requires significant physical interaction with unpredictable, often distressed animals, making automation of the core 'hands-on' tasks extremely difficult. While diagnostic data can be processed by AI, the necessity for human empathy in client communication and the high-stakes, real-time physical management of live patients creates a strong barrier to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Water and wastewater treatment plant and system operators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical labor and digital monitoring, with AI and advanced automation already contributing to a projected 7% decline in employment. While AI can optimize chemical dosing, analyze sensor data, and manage complex system flows, the role still requires physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sampling, and emergency response to mechanical failures.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the monitoring and chemical dosing can be digitized, the job requires significant physical intervention in unstructured, hazardous environments during equipment failure or emergencies. The high score for systemic accountability reflects the critical public health and legal consequences of water safety, which necessitates human oversight even if the primary process is automated.

rows.title: Flight attendants
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of flight attendants are physical and interpersonal, involving safety enforcement, emergency medical response, and manual service tasks that AI cannot perform. While AI may assist with peripheral digital tasks like report writing or passenger manifest management, the role is largely defined by a physical presence required by law for passenger safety.
rows.new_rationale: While service tasks are routine, the core of the role involves high-stakes physical intervention in an unpredictable, confined environment where AI lacks the physical agency to manage emergencies or unruly passengers. The requirement for human presence is legally mandated and socially essential, as the role relies on human authority, empathy, and physical liability that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rows.old_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.
rows.new_rationale: While the creation of press releases, campaign strategies, and digital content has high agentic potential for AI execution, the core value of these roles relies on high-stakes human trust, relationship building with major donors, and the moral authority required to manage organizational reputation during crises. The necessity for human-to-human rapport and the legal/ethical accountability for public statements create a significant barrier to full automation, despite the high degree of digital output.

rows.title: Insurance underwriters
rows.old_rationale: Underwriting is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on routine information processing, risk modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI excels. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to automation, and the shift from traditional software to advanced AI will likely automate even complex, non-standard risk assessments that previously required human judgment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an underwriter is a digital decision (approval/premium rate) based on data, which is already heavily automated and highly susceptible to full agentic execution. While complex cases require synthesis, the work environment is almost entirely controlled and office-based, leaving systemic accountability as the primary barrier to total automation, as legal and financial liability for high-stakes underwriting decisions remains a human-centric requirement.

rows.title: Architects
rows.old_rationale: Architects perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, utilizing CADD and BIM software which is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation in generative design and technical documentation. While the role requires physical site visits and complex client negotiations, a significant portion of the core work—drafting, specification writing, and regulatory compliance—is moving toward high AI integration.
rows.new_rationale: While architects produce digital artifacts (BIM/CADD) that are increasingly AI-generative, the final output must interface with highly unpredictable, unstructured physical construction sites where real-world variables often deviate from digital models. The role remains anchored by high systemic accountability, as the architect bears legal and ethical liability for the structural integrity and safety of the built environment, which cannot be fully offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and manual, involving the handling, feeding, and restraining of live animals in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or diagnostic data entry, it cannot replace the physical presence and manual dexterity required for animal care, sterilization, and surgical assistance.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in physical interaction with living, unpredictable animals, which requires real-time, tactile, and empathetic responses that software cannot replicate. While some tasks like monitoring are routine, the high-stakes physical environment—handling scared or aggressive animals—creates a significant barrier to full automation, as the 'output' is the physical safety and care of a biological entity rather than a digital artifact.

rows.title: Editors
rows.old_rationale: Editing is a fully digital occupation centered on language processing, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already perform high-level copy editing, fact-checking, and rewriting, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human editors required for routine content management.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an editor is digital text and content management, which is highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of synthesis and proofing. While high-level editorial judgment and the human-to-human coaching of writers retain some necessity, the primary tasks are increasingly delegatable to software, and the work environment is almost entirely controlled and digital.

rows.title: Operations research analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data collection, mathematical modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI and Large Language Models excel. While analysts still provide high-level human judgment and stakeholder persuasion, AI can now automate the core technical tasks of coding analytical tools, mining data, and generating optimization simulations, significantly increasing individual productivity and restructuring the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital—models, reports, and strategic recommendations—which are highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of processing vast datasets. While the work requires high-level synthesis, it occurs in highly controlled, office-based environments, and the human element is largely limited to stakeholder persuasion and final accountability for high-stakes organizational decisions.

rows.title: Advertising sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing sales statistics, preparing media kits, and drafting contracts, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal relationship building, the shift toward programmatic advertising and AI-driven ad placement is already reducing the need for human intermediaries in the sales process, as reflected in the projected employment decline.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—identifying leads, presenting media kits, and drafting contracts—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation via AI agents that can personalize outreach and execute transactions. While human rapport is currently used to close deals, the job lacks high-stakes physical or systemic accountability, making the human element increasingly optional as AI-driven programmatic advertising and automated sales funnels mature.

rows.title: Pest control workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring workers to crawl into tight spaces, set manual traps, and apply chemicals in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like route optimization, pest identification from photos, or automated billing, the primary labor cannot be performed digitally or remotely.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical intervention in highly unstructured, variable environments (crawlspaces, attics, and diverse building structures) that are currently beyond the reach of autonomous robotics. While the cognitive tasks are relatively routine, the high-stakes nature of applying toxic chemicals in residential settings creates significant systemic accountability and legal liability that necessitates human oversight.

rows.title: Chemists and materials scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a high-level blend of digital knowledge work and physical laboratory experimentation. AI is already revolutionizing the digital aspects—such as molecular modeling, predictive simulation, and technical reporting—allowing scientists to screen millions of compounds in silico before ever entering a lab. While the physical requirement of conducting experiments and handling chemicals provides a buffer, the massive productivity gains in research and analysis mean AI will fundamentally restructure how these scientists spend their time.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly handle the modeling and simulation of chemical properties, the final output often requires physical synthesis and validation in unpredictable, non-digitized laboratory environments. The high score for systemic accountability reflects the critical nature of chemical safety, pharmaceutical efficacy, and forensic integrity, where human oversight remains a non-delegatable legal and ethical requirement.

rows.title: Electrical and electronic engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as assembling prototypes, soldering circuitry, and repairing hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, a substantial portion of the role involves digital tasks like drafting schematics, analyzing test data, and writing technical reports—all areas where AI is becoming highly proficient. AI will likely augment their troubleshooting capabilities and automate the documentation aspects of the job, increasing individual productivity but potentially reducing overall demand.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the work involves physical assembly and repair in varied environments—which limits full automation—the core tasks of testing, calibration, and diagnostic reporting are increasingly susceptible to AI-driven robotic agents. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the critical nature of electrical safety and infrastructure reliability, where human oversight remains essential for liability and risk management despite the technical nature of the output.

rows.title: Rehabilitation counselors
rows.old_rationale: Rehabilitation counseling involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as developing rehabilitation plans, maintaining records, and researching resources, which are highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the profession relies on high-stakes interpersonal interaction, empathy, and physical advocacy for clients with disabilities, creating a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks and resource matching have some digital potential, the core value of the role is rooted in the human-to-human therapeutic bond, empathy, and advocacy, which cannot be delegated to an agent. The work requires high-dimensional synthesis of a client's psychological, physical, and social needs, and the counselor holds significant ethical and legal accountability for the life-altering decisions made regarding a client's rehabilitation and independence.

rows.title: Diagnostic medical sonographers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical and digital work; while AI is rapidly advancing in image analysis and diagnostic assistance, the role requires significant physical presence to position patients and manipulate transducers with high hand-eye coordination. Furthermore, the interpersonal aspect of patient care and the real-time physical adjustments needed during a scan provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly automate image interpretation and guidance, the job requires physical manipulation of a transducer on a patient's body to capture optimal diagnostic views, which remains a high-stakes, real-time physical task. The necessity for human interaction to manage patient comfort, physical positioning, and the legal/ethical accountability for diagnostic accuracy ensures that the human remains central to the process despite the digital nature of the final image.

rows.title: Interior designers
rows.old_rationale: Interior design is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD and BIM, which are being rapidly transformed by generative AI capable of creating photorealistic renderings and floor plans from text prompts. While the job requires physical site visits, material inspections, and complex interpersonal coordination with contractors, the core creative and technical drafting tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design process (CAD, BIM, material specification) is increasingly digital and agentic, the role requires high-level synthesis of aesthetic, functional, and regulatory constraints. The physical environment remains unpredictable during site visits and construction oversight, and the core value relies heavily on interpreting subjective human needs and maintaining professional liability for safety and building code compliance.

rows.title: Veterinarians
rows.old_rationale: Veterinary work is a hybrid of physical procedures and complex diagnostic knowledge work. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays/ultrasounds) and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical surgery, manual examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication with pet owners that cannot be automated.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics, the core output of veterinary medicine is physical intervention—surgery, wound care, and handling unpredictable, often distressed animals in varied environments—which remains largely beyond the reach of autonomous agents. Furthermore, the role requires deep human empathy for client communication and carries significant ethical and legal liability for life-or-death decisions that cannot be offloaded to software.

rows.title: Ironworkers
rows.old_rationale: Ironworking is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable, outdoor environments and at great heights, which provides a significant natural barrier to AI and robotics. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or site scheduling, the core duties of manual welding, bolting, and balancing on structural beams require real-time human dexterity and physical presence that cannot be automated with current or near-future technology.
rows.new_rationale: The work is fundamentally physical, requiring interaction with heavy, volatile materials in unstructured, high-altitude environments that are currently beyond the capability of autonomous agents. While the cognitive tasks involve blueprint interpretation, the primary value is the physical execution and the high-stakes liability associated with structural integrity, which necessitates human oversight and accountability.

rows.title: Biological technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate data interpretation, report writing, and experimental modeling, the core duties involve manual tasks like setting up equipment, preparing biological samples, and conducting physical experiments that require a human presence in a lab or field environment.
rows.new_rationale: Much of the technician's work involves standardized laboratory protocols and data collection that are highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven robotic platforms. While some roles involve unpredictable field environments, the core function is procedural and technical, requiring human oversight primarily for safety and accountability rather than unique human judgment or empathy.

rows.title: Small engine mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of mechanical components, manual dexterity, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with diagnostic software and technical documentation, it cannot perform the physical labor of disassembling engines, replacing parts, or conducting hands-on repairs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical manipulation of hardware in unstructured, often cramped or outdoor environments, which remains highly resistant to current robotic automation. While diagnostics involve cognitive synthesis, the final output is a physical repair that requires human dexterity and sensory feedback, making the agentic potential for digital-only execution extremely low.

rows.title: Railroad workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, mechanical adjustment, and real-world safety monitoring that AI cannot perform. However, core functions like train operation, scheduling, and signal management are increasingly susceptible to automation and AI-driven efficiency systems, which the BLS notes is already limiting employment growth.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical operation of trains is increasingly amenable to automation, the high-stakes nature of rail transport creates a massive barrier to full autonomy due to extreme systemic accountability and the need for human intervention in unpredictable, high-consequence physical environments. The role remains heavily constrained by safety regulations and the requirement for human liability in the event of catastrophic failure, despite the technical feasibility of agentic control over the train's movement.

rows.title: Marriage and family therapists
rows.old_rationale: While the work is increasingly digital via telehealth and involves significant information processing, the core value of therapy lies in human-to-human empathy, trust, and the navigation of complex interpersonal dynamics. AI will likely automate administrative tasks and provide diagnostic support, but the high-stakes emotional nature of family and marriage counseling provides a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with documentation and basic cognitive behavioral techniques, the core value of this role is rooted in the human-to-human therapeutic alliance, which requires genuine empathy and shared lived experience that an agent cannot replicate. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for nuanced, real-time emotional navigation in a clinical setting make this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Industrial engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital work, such as analyzing production costs, using CAD/CAM software, and conducting statistical studies, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role also requires physical presence on the manufacturing floor to observe workers, assess machinery performance, and evaluate physical layouts, which provides a partial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—process layouts, workflow diagrams, and statistical reports—is highly digitizable and can be generated by AI agents integrated with CAD/CAM and ERP systems. While the work occurs in physical manufacturing environments, the technician's role is primarily observational and analytical rather than manual, making it highly susceptible to automation as sensor data replaces human observation.

rows.title: Health education specialists
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing community health data, writing grant proposals, and creating educational materials, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core requirements of community advocacy, empathetic interpersonal interaction, and physical presence for local events and training provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The majority of the output—educational materials, program strategies, and data analysis—is highly digital and easily generated or executed by AI agents. However, the core value of the role relies heavily on human-to-human trust, empathy, and the ability to navigate complex social dynamics in community settings, which remains a significant barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Aerospace engineers
rows.old_rationale: Aerospace engineering is a high-level knowledge occupation where core tasks like aerodynamic modeling, structural analysis, and propulsion design are increasingly performed using digital simulation and AI-driven optimization tools. While the role requires critical human judgment for safety, regulatory compliance, and physical inspection of prototypes, the heavy reliance on computer-aided design (CAD) and complex data analysis makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task automation.
rows.new_rationale: While aerospace engineers produce digital models and simulations that are highly automatable, the final output must interface with complex, high-stakes physical environments where failure is catastrophic. The requirement for professional licensure and the legal liability associated with safety-critical systems create a high barrier to full delegation, as human oversight remains essential for ethical and regulatory accountability.

rows.title: Medical equipment repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical and manual, requiring dexterity to handle tools, disassemble machinery, and work in tight physical spaces. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software, troubleshooting, and predictive maintenance scheduling, the physical act of repairing, installing, and calibrating hardware remains a human-centric task that cannot be automated by digital AI alone.
rows.new_rationale: While software diagnostics and calibration are increasingly automated, the physical nature of the work—disassembling heavy, complex machinery in varied clinical environments—remains a significant barrier to full agentic replacement. The high-stakes nature of life-supporting equipment creates a critical accountability threshold where human verification is legally and ethically required, even if AI assists in the diagnostic process.

rows.title: Insulation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable, confined environments like attics and crawl spaces. AI has minimal impact on the physical installation of materials, though it may slightly assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or material estimation.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity in highly unstructured, confined, and hazardous environments that are currently beyond the reach of autonomous robotics. While the cognitive tasks are relatively straightforward, the systemic accountability for fire-safety compliance and hazardous material handling (like asbestos) necessitates human oversight, making full automation of the final physical output extremely difficult.

rows.title: Travel agents
rows.old_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a travel agent is entirely digital—itineraries, bookings, and reservations—which is highly susceptible to automation by AI agents that can interface directly with global distribution systems. While the job requires some synthesis of client preferences, the work environment is entirely controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical or high-stakes accountability that would necessitate a human presence.

rows.title: Labor relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the drafting of contracts, policy development, and legal compliance—all areas where AI excels. While the high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and dispute resolution provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document generation, and investigative analysis components of the role.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the documentation and policy drafting is highly automatable, the core value of the role lies in high-stakes human mediation, trust-building, and the navigation of volatile interpersonal dynamics that require human presence. The occupation carries significant legal and ethical accountability for labor disputes and contract enforcement, making it difficult to fully delegate to an autonomous agent despite the high potential for digital output.

rows.title: Financial examiners
rows.old_rationale: Financial examiners perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis of balance sheets, loan documentation, and regulatory text. AI is exceptionally well-suited for automating the core tasks of this role, such as identifying anomalies in financial data, summarizing meeting minutes, and checking compliance against complex legal frameworks, though human judgment remains necessary for final risk assessments and high-level management evaluations.
rows.new_rationale: Financial examination is highly data-centric, making the output almost entirely digital and ripe for AI-driven auditing and compliance monitoring. While the work requires high-level analytical synthesis, the physical environment is highly controlled, and the primary barrier to full automation is the systemic requirement for human legal and ethical accountability in regulatory enforcement.

rows.title: Cardiovascular technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role requires significant physical presence for tasks like positioning patients, attaching electrodes, and assisting in invasive surgical procedures. While AI will heavily automate the analysis of EKG and pulmonary data, the core requirements of patient care, physical stamina, and real-time technical assistance in a clinical environment provide a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic data produced is digital, the core of the role involves high-stakes physical interaction with patients, including invasive procedures and patient positioning, which limits agentic output potential. The necessity for human empathy, physical bedside care, and the critical legal/ethical accountability for patient safety during invasive procedures creates a high barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Glaziers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of a glazier is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or precision measurements, the actual installation, cutting, and securing of glass in the physical world remain well beyond current AI and robotic capabilities.
rows.new_rationale: The work of a glazier is fundamentally physical, requiring the manipulation of heavy, fragile materials in unstructured, high-altitude, or volatile construction environments that are highly resistant to current robotic automation.
