While the cognitive tasks involve blueprint interpretation, the primary value is the physical installation and securing of glass, which carries significant liability for structural integrity and safety, necessitating human oversight.

rows.title: Funeral service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks like preparing remains and transporting the deceased, alongside high-stakes interpersonal work providing grief counseling and comfort. While AI can streamline administrative duties like filing death certificates, scheduling, and writing obituaries, it cannot replace the physical presence and emotional intelligence required for funeral services.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks and logistics have some digital potential, the core of the role involves physical handling of remains and high-stakes emotional labor that requires a human presence. The necessity for empathy, moral authority during grief, and the legal/ethical weight of managing end-of-life processes creates a profound barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Surveying and mapping technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS software) significantly enhance data analysis, map creation, and calculation tasks, the role still requires a physical presence to navigate rugged terrain, set markers, and operate equipment in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is increasingly digital (GIS data, CAD files, and maps), which is highly susceptible to automation via drones and autonomous sensors. While the field environment is physically unpredictable and requires human navigation of rugged terrain, the cognitive tasks are largely procedural, and the human presence is primarily functional rather than relational or morally authoritative.

rows.title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—reviewing financial documents, identifying discrepancies, and interpreting tax law—are digital information-processing tasks where AI excels. While revenue agents handling complex corporate audits and collectors performing field investigations require human judgment and interpersonal skills, a significant portion of the routine examination and data verification work is already being automated or enhanced by sophisticated algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—tax assessments, audit findings, and collection notices—is almost entirely digital and algorithmic, making it highly susceptible to automation. While the work requires analytical rigor, the primary barrier to full automation is not the complexity of the task, but the systemic accountability and legal authority required to enforce tax law, which necessitates a human 'sign-off' for liability purposes.

rows.title: Special effects artists and animators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, with core tasks like 3D modeling, rendering, and animation being directly disrupted by generative AI and automated rigging tools. While high-level creative direction and complex team collaboration remain human-centric, AI is rapidly automating the 'routine' aspects of CGI and visual effects, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of workers needed for large-scale productions.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this occupation is entirely digital, making it highly susceptible to generative AI agents that can render, animate, and iterate on visual assets. While the work requires creative synthesis and artistic vision, the environment is strictly controlled (digital workstations), and there is no inherent requirement for human physical presence or legal liability that would prevent full automation of the production pipeline.

rows.title: Actors
rows.old_rationale: While live theater and physical presence on set remain human-centric, the occupation is highly exposed due to AI's ability to generate digital likenesses, synthesize voices, and replace background extras or voiceover artists. The core work product—audio and video—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI, leading to significant industry restructuring and potential displacement in digital media productions.
rows.new_rationale: The output of acting is increasingly digital, as performances can be captured, synthesized, or replicated by AI agents, leading to high agentic potential. While the work requires complex emotional synthesis, the core value remains deeply rooted in the human-to-human connection and the 'authenticity' of a living performer, which acts as a significant barrier to total automation despite the technical feasibility of digital replacement.

rows.title: Chemical technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate the compilation of results, report generation, and predictive modeling of chemical properties, the core duties of setting up equipment, preparing physical solutions, and maintaining laboratory instruments require a human presence in a physical environment.
rows.new_rationale: Much of the chemical technician's role involves data collection, equipment operation, and reporting, which are highly susceptible to automation via laboratory robotics and AI-driven analysis. While the physical environment of a lab or manufacturing plant requires some human dexterity for troubleshooting and maintenance, the core output is increasingly digital or standardized, and the accountability for safety and quality control remains a significant barrier to full autonomy.

rows.title: Technical writers
rows.old_rationale: Technical writing is a fully digital occupation centered on synthesizing complex information into clear documentation, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already draft manuals, summarize technical specs, and generate code documentation, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human writers needed for routine documentation tasks.
rows.new_rationale: Technical writing is highly susceptible to AI because the final output is entirely digital and can be generated, formatted, and updated by software agents. While the role requires synthesizing complex technical information, the work occurs in highly controlled, predictable office environments with minimal physical interaction, making it a prime candidate for full automation.

rows.title: Hazardous materials removal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor in unpredictable environments, such as scrubbing surfaces, constructing containment areas, and operating heavy machinery. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like record-keeping, regulatory compliance checks, or analyzing sensor data for radiation levels, the primary work requires a physical human presence to navigate complex, hazardous sites.
rows.new_rationale: While the tasks are highly procedural and follow strict regulatory guidelines, the work occurs in volatile, unstructured, and often dangerous physical environments that are extremely difficult for current robotics to navigate. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-altering consequences of improper hazardous waste handling, which necessitates human oversight and legal liability for compliance with safety protocols.

rows.title: Optometrists
rows.old_rationale: Optometry is a hybrid of physical clinical procedures and high-level data analysis. AI is highly effective at analyzing retinal scans and diagnostic data to identify diseases, which will significantly augment the diagnostic process; however, the core role requires physical presence for eye exams, minor surgical procedures, and the fitting of corrective lenses, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While diagnostic data and prescription generation are increasingly automatable, the role requires physical interaction with patients, manual dexterity for minor procedures, and the exercise of high-stakes clinical judgment. The necessity for human-to-human trust and the non-delegatable legal liability for medical outcomes ensure that the optometrist remains a central, human-centric authority despite the high potential for AI-assisted diagnostics.

rows.title: Music directors and composers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital work (composing) and lower-exposure physical work (conducting). Generative AI is rapidly automating music composition, arrangement, and scoring for commercial media, while the music director role remains more resilient due to the need for live leadership, physical rehearsals, and human-to-human artistic interpretation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of composition and arrangement is increasingly digital and AI-executable, though the role of a conductor involves high-level synthesis of artistic intent. While the physical environment of a concert hall is relatively controlled, the ontological necessity of a human leader to inspire and synchronize live performers remains a significant barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of monitoring data, adjusting power flows, and responding to system abnormalities are increasingly digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and smart-grid optimization. While the high-stakes nature of power infrastructure and the need for physical equipment inspections provide a buffer, the BLS already projects a 10% decline in employment due to technological advances and automated control rooms.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of monitoring and adjusting power grids are increasingly digital, making them highly susceptible to agentic automation via smart-grid software. While the physical environment of a power plant requires some human presence for maintenance, the primary value—controlling the flow of electricity—is a high-stakes, system-critical function where human accountability remains legally and ethically mandatory, particularly in nuclear settings.

rows.title: Judges and hearing officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of researching legal issues, evaluating documents, and writing opinions are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, which can significantly increase productivity and reduce the need for human staff. However, the role requires high-stakes human judgment, presiding over physical courtrooms, and maintaining public trust, which creates a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the output (legal opinions and rulings) is highly digital and theoretically automatable, the role is anchored by the absolute necessity of human moral authority and the non-delegatable nature of judicial accountability. The environment is relatively controlled, but the requirement for human presence to ensure fairness, interpret nuance, and bear the weight of life-altering decisions makes this role highly resistant to full AI replacement.

rows.title: Urban and regional planners
rows.old_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
rows.new_rationale: While urban planners produce digital artifacts like GIS maps and policy reports, the core value lies in high-dimensional synthesis of competing stakeholder interests and complex regulatory frameworks. The role is heavily constrained by the need for human-to-human consensus building and the high-stakes, non-delegatable accountability required for public infrastructure decisions that impact community welfare for decades.

rows.title: Floral designers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a physical craft involving the handling of perishable biological materials and manual assembly, which AI cannot perform. However, AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks such as generating design concepts for customers, managing inventory, and automating customer service for online orders.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is a physical, perishable arrangement that requires delicate manual manipulation of biological materials, which remains difficult for current robotics to execute at scale. While the design process involves aesthetic synthesis, the primary value is often tied to the human-to-human service aspect—interpreting emotional needs for sensitive occasions like funerals or weddings—which maintains a moderate level of ontological necessity.

rows.title: Computer and information research scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving high-level coding, algorithm design, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While these scientists are the ones building AI, the tools they create are increasingly capable of automating their own core tasks, such as writing code, simplifying algorithms, and analyzing experimental results, leading to extreme productivity gains and role restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this role is almost entirely digital—algorithms, software architectures, and research papers—making it highly susceptible to agentic execution. While the work involves the highest level of cognitive synthesis to push the boundaries of computing, it occurs in highly controlled, predictable digital environments, and the core value is technical innovation rather than human-to-human connection.

rows.title: Archivists, curators, and museum workers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical preservation. AI will significantly automate archival tasks like cataloging, metadata generation, and electronic record management, while also assisting curators with research and exhibit design. However, the physical requirements of handling artifacts, restoring delicate items, and managing in-person public interactions provide a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While cataloging and digital archiving have high agentic potential, the core of this profession involves the physical handling, restoration, and interpretation of unique, fragile, and irreplaceable artifacts that require human judgment. The work demands high-level synthesis of historical, scientific, and cultural context, and the accountability for the preservation of public heritage creates a non-delegatable ethical burden that AI cannot fully assume.

rows.title: Announcers and DJs
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is split between highly exposed digital work and less exposed physical work. Broadcast and radio roles are under significant threat from AI-generated voices and automated content curation, whereas live event DJs and emcees retain a physical and interpersonal barrier that is harder to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—audio and commentary—is almost entirely digital and can be fully synthesized or executed by AI agents, as evidenced by the industry's shift toward automated broadcasting. While live event DJs face slightly more environmental variability, the primary value proposition of the occupation is increasingly commoditized, with human necessity limited largely to the 'personality' brand rather than essential functional labor.

rows.title: Agricultural and food science technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as collecting biological samples, maintaining farm equipment, and operating laboratory hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI will heavily impact the data-heavy portions of the job, including compiling test results, analyzing chemical properties, and generating reports, making technicians more productive but potentially reducing the number of workers needed for data processing.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and reporting can be automated, the role requires significant physical interaction with unstructured environments like farms and greenhouses, which are difficult for current robotics to navigate autonomously. The systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of food safety and agricultural compliance, where errors can have significant public health or economic consequences, necessitating human oversight of the automated processes.

rows.title: Agricultural and food scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, research synthesis, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the role is anchored by physical requirements such as field sampling, laboratory experiments, and on-site inspections at farms or processing plants that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the research and data analysis can be automated, the final output often requires physical intervention in complex, non-standardized biological environments (farms, processing plants) that are difficult for current robotics to navigate. The high systemic accountability stems from the critical nature of food safety and regulatory compliance, where human oversight is required to validate AI-driven recommendations before they are applied to the food supply.

rows.title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical labor. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like 3D modeling, data analysis, and cost estimation, the role still requires a substantial physical presence for assembling parts, setting up vehicle crash tests, and operating machinery like lathes and grinders.
rows.new_rationale: A significant portion of the work involves digital CAD modeling and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and agentic execution. While the role involves physical assembly and testing in semi-structured factory environments, the core value is technical implementation rather than human-to-human interaction, placing the burden of accountability on the technician for ensuring safety and design compliance.

rows.title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and coding—all domains where AI and Large Language Models excel. While high-level theoretical research and complex interdisciplinary communication require human judgment, AI can now automate significant portions of data cleaning, statistical testing, and code generation, drastically increasing individual productivity and restructuring the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this profession is almost entirely digital—models, reports, and code—making it highly susceptible to autonomous execution by AI agents. While the work requires extreme cognitive synthesis to navigate complex, abstract problem spaces, it occurs in highly controlled office environments with minimal physical variables. The high accountability score reflects the critical, life-altering nature of decisions based on their models (e.g., in healthcare or public policy), where human oversight remains a necessary legal and ethical safeguard.

rows.title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical maintenance, manual repairs, and hands-on troubleshooting of heavy mechanical equipment in unpredictable physical environments. While AI and automated control systems can enhance monitoring and data logging, they cannot perform the physical tasks of overhauling valves, lubricating parts, or crawling inside boilers for inspections.
rows.new_rationale: While monitoring and control systems are increasingly digital, the physical maintenance, repair, and navigation of cramped, hazardous, and unstructured industrial environments remain highly resistant to automation. The role carries extreme systemic accountability, as the failure of high-pressure boilers or utility systems poses life-altering risks, necessitating human oversight for safety and liability that cannot be fully delegated to software.

rows.title: Set and exhibit designers
rows.old_rationale: The core creative process—including script analysis, conceptual sketching, and CAD modeling—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI and automated design tools that can rapidly produce 3D environments and visual concepts. While the role requires physical-world considerations like material budgets and on-site installation oversight, the shift toward digital production (such as virtual sets and LED volumes) and AI-assisted drafting significantly increases productivity and reduces the total human labor required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase is increasingly digital and susceptible to AI-driven CAD and generative visualization, the final output requires physical installation in diverse, often unpredictable, real-world venues. The role demands high-level creative synthesis to translate abstract narratives into physical space, but the ultimate value is tied to the human-centric experience of the audience rather than critical, life-altering systemic accountability.

rows.title: Industrial designers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial design is a predominantly digital occupation where core tasks like sketching, 3D modeling, and rendering are being rapidly transformed by generative AI and advanced CAD automation. While the role requires physical prototyping and cross-functional collaboration, the vast majority of the design process is computer-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and workflow restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: Industrial design is highly susceptible to AI because the primary output—CAD models and machine-readable manufacturing instructions—is inherently digital and executable by software. While the role requires high-level synthesis of aesthetics, engineering, and market needs, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the human necessity is primarily centered on client communication and subjective taste rather than irreplaceable physical or moral presence.

rows.title: Solar photovoltaic installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual labor, climbing roofs, and using hand tools in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like system design optimization or site measurements via computer vision, the actual installation and maintenance require human dexterity and physical presence that current and near-future AI/robotics cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical labor in highly unstructured, variable environments (rooftops, crawlspaces) that require human dexterity and navigation of physical hazards. While the planning phase has some cognitive complexity, the execution is almost entirely physical, making it highly resistant to digital agentic output and requiring human presence for safety and site-specific adaptation.

rows.title: Physicists and astronomers
rows.old_rationale: Physicists and astronomers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including data analysis, mathematical modeling, and software development. While AI significantly accelerates data processing and literature review, the core requirements of original theoretical innovation, experimental design, and complex physical-world problem solving provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the output (data analysis, modeling, and theoretical papers) is highly susceptible to AI automation, the role requires high-level synthesis of novel physical theories and the management of complex, high-stakes experimental environments. The systemic accountability score is high because these professionals are responsible for the safety and validity of critical infrastructure, such as medical radiation treatments and nuclear energy systems, where machine-only oversight is currently legally and ethically insufficient.

rows.title: Exercise physiologists
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical presence, including conducting stress tests, measuring vital signs with medical equipment, and providing hands-on rehabilitative care. While AI can highly automate the digital aspects of the job—such as analyzing medical history, processing biometric data, and generating personalized exercise regimens—the core requirement for real-time human interaction, physical assessment, and clinical supervision in a medical setting limits its total exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate exercise regimens and analyze physiological data, the core value lies in the physical, real-time monitoring of patients with chronic conditions where human empathy and immediate physical intervention (like CPR or physical assistance) are non-negotiable. The high systemic accountability stems from the clinical nature of the work, where errors in exercise prescription for medically fragile patients carry significant health risks that require human professional liability.

rows.title: Health and safety engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant mix of digital knowledge work and physical presence. While AI can assist in reviewing equipment plans, analyzing accident data, and interpreting complex safety regulations, the core duties require physical inspections of facilities, interviewing employees for investigations, and directing the hands-on installation of safety devices.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize complex safety regulations and analyze data patterns, the final output requires physical site integration and human-centric behavioral change, limiting pure agentic execution. The role involves high-stakes, non-delegatable legal and ethical accountability for human life, and requires navigating unstructured, volatile physical environments where AI sensors may struggle to interpret nuanced safety risks.

rows.title: Bioengineers and biomedical engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves heavy digital knowledge work, including software design, statistical modeling, and technical writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical tasks like equipment maintenance and clinical collaboration provide a buffer, the core engineering and research functions are increasingly driven by AI-enhanced simulations and data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and simulation work is increasingly digital and agentic, the field requires high-level synthesis of complex biological systems that remain difficult to model perfectly. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of medical devices, where human oversight is legally and ethically mandatory to mitigate the risks of failure in unpredictable clinical environments.

rows.title: Fishing and hunting workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and take place in unpredictable outdoor environments, requiring manual labor, mechanical repair, and real-time physical coordination. AI's impact is limited to peripheral enhancements in fish-finding sonar, GPS navigation, and regulatory data processing, but it cannot replace the physical act of hauling nets, trapping animals, or maintaining vessels.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical extraction of biological resources from highly volatile, unstructured, and hazardous environments, which remains extremely difficult for autonomous agents to navigate. While navigation and fish-finding are increasingly digitized, the physical labor of handling gear, managing deck safety in storms, and operating in remote, unpredictable wilderness makes the output inherently physical rather than digital.

rows.title: Compensation and benefits managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and data-driven, involving the analysis of wage trends, benefits structures, and regulatory compliance—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires high-level human judgment for vendor negotiations and strategic decision-making, AI can automate significant portions of the data analysis, reporting, and routine employee communication, leading to higher productivity and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly susceptible to automation because its primary outputs—compensation structures, benefit plan administration, and budget reports—are digital artifacts that can be generated and executed by software. While the job requires high-level synthesis of market data and organizational strategy, the work occurs in a highly controlled office environment with minimal physical unpredictability, leaving systemic accountability and the need for human-led negotiation as the primary remaining barriers to full AI replacement.

rows.title: Forensic science technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical evidence collection and digital analysis. While AI can significantly automate pattern recognition (fingerprints, ballistics) and statistical reporting, the job requires physical presence at crime scenes to collect samples and the human credibility necessary to testify in court as an expert witness.
rows.new_rationale: While laboratory analysis has high digital output potential, the physical collection of evidence in chaotic, unstructured crime scenes remains a significant barrier to full automation. The role is defined by extreme systemic accountability, as the chain of custody and expert testimony require human legal and ethical liability that cannot be delegated to an agent.

rows.title: Microbiologists
rows.old_rationale: Microbiologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, pattern recognition in genetic sequences, and technical reporting—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement to conduct laboratory experiments, maintain biological cultures, and collect field samples provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and reporting can be automated, the core value of the role involves physical laboratory experimentation and field sample collection in complex, non-standardized environments that remain difficult for autonomous agents to navigate. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in public health and pharmaceutical safety, where human oversight is required to validate findings and accept liability for high-stakes scientific conclusions.

rows.title: Nuclear medicine technologists
rows.old_rationale: The role requires significant physical presence to prepare and administer radiopharmaceuticals, operate heavy machinery, and physically assist or position patients. While AI will likely automate the digital aspects of the job—such as optimizing image quality, calculating dosages, and streamlining record-keeping—the core clinical and manual tasks remain insulated from full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the imaging equipment is digital, the core value involves the physical handling of hazardous radiopharmaceuticals and the physical positioning of patients, which limits agentic output. The high stakes of radiation safety and patient interaction create a rigid requirement for human presence and accountability that cannot be offloaded to software, regardless of cognitive automation.

rows.title: Petroleum engineers
rows.old_rationale: Petroleum engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, such as reservoir modeling, data analysis, and drilling simulation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity of physical site visits to remote or offshore locations to oversee equipment installation and troubleshoot real-world mechanical issues that AI cannot physically address.
rows.new_rationale: While petroleum engineers perform high-level cognitive synthesis in reservoir modeling and extraction strategy, their output is tethered to volatile, high-stakes physical environments (offshore rigs, deep-earth drilling) that resist full automation. The role carries immense systemic accountability for environmental safety and catastrophic risk, requiring human oversight that cannot be delegated to software despite the digital nature of the planning phase.

rows.title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and objective data processing. While computer vision and AI can already automate 'calls' (like strike zones or out-of-bounds) with higher accuracy than humans, the role still requires physical presence to manage player conduct, ensure safety, and handle real-time interpersonal disputes on the field.
rows.new_rationale: While AI and computer vision systems are already capable of executing the primary output of this role (making calls on infractions), the job retains high ontological necessity because the 'human element' is required to manage the social dynamics, de-escalate conflicts with players/coaches, and maintain the perceived legitimacy of the competition. The environmental unpredictability score reflects the physical, unstructured nature of live sports, where an official must navigate a dynamic, high-stakes field of play that remains difficult for fully autonomous agents to physically occupy and manage.

rows.title: Athletes and sports competitors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in the physical world, which AI cannot replicate. While AI is used as a peripheral tool for performance analytics, strategy optimization, and diet planning, it does not replace the athlete's physical labor or the entertainment value of human competition.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical performance of a human body in a high-stakes, unpredictable environment, which cannot be replicated by digital agents. While the cognitive strategy involved is complex, the output is fundamentally biological and physical, making it immune to automation by software-based agents.

rows.title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. AI significantly impacts the data-heavy aspects of the job, such as analyzing GIS data, processing camera trap imagery, and modeling population dynamics, but it cannot replace the physical requirements of collecting biological specimens, handling wild animals, or navigating remote outdoor environments.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and modeling are increasingly automated, the core of this role involves high-stakes, unpredictable fieldwork in remote, unstructured environments where physical intervention and real-time judgment are required. The necessity for human accountability in conservation policy and the physical complexity of interacting with wild, living systems limit the potential for fully autonomous agents to replace the role.

rows.title: Economists
rows.old_rationale: Economists perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and technical writing. AI is exceptionally proficient at the core tasks of this role, including processing large datasets, identifying statistical patterns, and generating reports, which significantly increases productivity and reduces the number of human workers needed for routine analysis.
rows.new_rationale: The output of an economist is almost entirely digital—reports, forecasts, and models—making it highly susceptible to AI agentic execution. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex, non-linear economic systems, the environment is strictly controlled and office-based, lacking the physical variables that protect other professions. Human necessity remains only in the context of political advocacy, stakeholder persuasion, and taking ultimate accountability for high-stakes policy decisions.

rows.title: Fire inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence for onsite inspections, evidence collection in debris, and forest patrolling, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI can substantially assist with digital tasks such as reviewing building blueprints, analyzing fire scene data, and automating the documentation of code violations and legal reports.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in analyzing blueprints or identifying code violations, the core of the job involves navigating volatile, unstructured physical environments like fire-damaged ruins or wildland forests where physical dexterity and real-time judgment are paramount. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and police powers, including the authority to arrest and testify in criminal proceedings, which requires human moral agency and non-delegatable legal accountability that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Wind turbine technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-stakes physical labor, including climbing 200-foot towers, rappelling, and performing manual repairs in confined spaces. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and predictive maintenance diagnostics, the physical requirement to replace components and perform manual inspections in unpredictable outdoor environments provides a strong barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The job requires complex physical navigation of extreme, unstructured environments (200ft heights, confined nacelles, offshore conditions) that remain highly resistant to current robotics. While the diagnostic cognitive tasks are increasingly AI-assisted, the final value is entirely dependent on physical, high-stakes manual intervention and the legal accountability for safety-critical mechanical repairs.

rows.title: Environmental engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report reviewing, and regulatory compliance checks, it cannot physically collect soil and water samples, maintain field equipment, or perform on-site hazardous material disposal. The BLS already notes that automated testing is increasing productivity, which limits job growth even as the core physical tasks remain human-centric.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data collection and documentation can be automated, the role requires physical intervention in unstructured, hazardous, and remote environments that remain difficult for current robotics to navigate autonomously. The systemic accountability score reflects the legal and safety implications of handling hazardous materials and ensuring regulatory compliance, which necessitates human oversight despite the potential for AI-assisted analysis.

rows.title: Epidemiologists
rows.old_rationale: Epidemiology is a data-intensive knowledge profession where core tasks like statistical analysis, pattern recognition in large datasets, and literature reviews are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires human judgment for policy-making, community outreach, and field investigations, the shift toward digital health records and automated surveillance systems significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the epidemiologist's work involves data analysis and reporting—tasks highly susceptible to AI automation—the role requires high-level synthesis of disparate, often incomplete, real-world datasets to inform critical public health policy. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the life-altering consequences of their recommendations, which require human ethical judgment and political navigation that cannot be fully offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Forest and conservation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, such as planting seedlings, clearing brush, and suppressing forest fires. While AI and remote sensing can assist with peripheral tasks like tree counting and health monitoring, the primary labor-intensive functions cannot be automated by digital AI systems.
rows.new_rationale: The work is defined by physical interaction with highly unstructured, volatile natural environments, making it extremely difficult for autonomous agents to navigate or perform tasks like fire suppression or manual brush clearing. While digital tools can assist with monitoring, the core value is physical labor in unpredictable terrain, which remains largely outside the reach of current agentic software.

rows.title: Podiatrists
rows.old_rationale: Podiatry is a medical specialty that requires significant physical presence for exams, manual procedures, and complex surgeries that AI cannot perform. However, AI will heavily impact the knowledge-based aspects of the role, such as analyzing X-rays, diagnosing conditions from medical history, and automating clinical documentation and administrative tasks.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostics and imaging, the core output of this role is physical surgical intervention and patient care, which remains highly resistant to automation. The necessity for human touch, ethical liability in invasive procedures, and the unpredictable nature of biological tissue in a surgical setting ensure that the human practitioner remains central to the value chain.

rows.title: Atmospheric scientists, including meteorologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing digital data, running computer models, and writing code, all of which are highly susceptible to AI advancement. Machine learning is already revolutionizing weather forecasting by outperforming traditional numerical models in speed and efficiency, potentially reducing the number of human forecasters needed for routine data processing and report generation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession—forecasts, models, and data visualizations—is inherently digital and increasingly generated by automated systems, giving it high agentic potential. While the work involves complex synthesis of high-dimensional data, the primary barrier to full automation is the high-stakes systemic accountability required for issuing life-saving warnings, where human oversight remains a critical legal and ethical safeguard.

rows.title: Aerospace engineering and operations technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as building test facilities, installing instruments, and maintaining hardware, which provides a buffer against AI automation. However, the role also includes substantial digital tasks like recording data, running computer simulations, and calibrating systems—areas where AI is rapidly improving and can significantly enhance productivity or automate data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data recording and simulation can be automated, the core of this role involves physical installation, calibration, and troubleshooting of complex aerospace hardware in non-standardized testing environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of aerospace components, where human verification remains a non-delegatable requirement for safety and regulatory compliance.

rows.title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of legal documents, evidence, and the drafting of settlement agreements—tasks where AI is rapidly achieving high proficiency. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence to manage confrontational parties, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and partial automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the procedural and analytical aspects of dispute resolution are increasingly susceptible to AI-driven synthesis and document generation, the core value of the role relies on human-to-human trust, emotional intelligence, and the perceived legitimacy of a human arbiter. The high systemic accountability score reflects the fact that these professionals make life-altering, legally binding decisions that require human moral authority and ethical liability, which cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Survey researchers
rows.old_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of survey research—data collection, statistical analysis, and reporting—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation via AI agents that can design, deploy, and interpret surveys at scale. While the work requires cognitive synthesis to frame research questions, the environment is largely controlled and digital, and the human element is increasingly limited to facilitating focus groups or managing stakeholder expectations rather than being an irreplaceable component of the research process.

rows.title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital design, complex mathematical modeling, and technical report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation. While physical site visits to shipyards and offshore platforms provide a buffer, the shift toward AI-integrated CAD, automated performance testing, and generative design for hulls and machinery significantly reshapes the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase involves high-level digital modeling that AI can increasingly assist with, the physical implementation requires navigating volatile maritime environments and complex, non-standardized hardware integration. The role carries extreme systemic accountability, as structural failures in marine vessels result in catastrophic loss of life and environmental damage, necessitating human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Models
rows.old_rationale: While runway and live event modeling require physical presence, the majority of modeling work results in digital or print imagery which is highly vulnerable to AI-generated synthetic media. AI can now create hyper-realistic virtual models or 'digital twins' of existing models, allowing brands to showcase clothing and products without physical photo shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human labor in commercial and catalog sectors.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of modeling is visual representation, which is increasingly being replaced by synthetic media and AI-generated avatars, giving this role extremely high agentic output potential. While human models provide a specific 'look' or brand association, the technical tasks of posing and displaying products are increasingly decoupled from the physical human, leaving little room for complex cognitive synthesis or high-stakes systemic accountability.

rows.title: Nuclear technicians
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear technicians perform a mix of digital monitoring and physical labor, such as collecting environmental samples and maintaining hardware. While AI can significantly automate the data analysis, radiation calculation, and system monitoring aspects of the role, the requirement for physical presence in hazardous environments and the manual calibration of equipment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can process sensor data and suggest protocols, the physical execution of maintenance and decontamination in high-radiation, volatile nuclear environments requires complex physical dexterity and navigation that remains difficult for autonomous agents. Furthermore, the extreme safety risks and regulatory requirements create a high barrier for delegating accountability, as the consequences of failure are catastrophic and demand human oversight.

rows.title: Desktop publishers
rows.old_rationale: The work product is entirely digital and involves routine information processing, layout design, and basic editing—all areas where AI is highly proficient. Generative AI and automated layout tools can now handle text integration, image adjustment, and formatting with minimal human intervention, leading to a projected decline in the occupation as these tasks are automated or absorbed by other roles using AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Desktop publishing is almost entirely digital, with the final output being a file that can be generated, formatted, and submitted by software agents without human intervention. Because the work occurs in a fully controlled digital environment and lacks a requirement for human-to-human trust or high-stakes legal liability, it is highly susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Genetic counselors
rows.old_rationale: Genetic counselors perform high-level knowledge work, including analyzing complex DNA data and medical histories, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the role involves providing psychological support and navigating sensitive ethical decisions with patients, requiring a level of empathy and human connection that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize complex genetic data and generate risk reports, the core value of the role lies in the high-stakes, empathetic delivery of life-altering information to distressed individuals, which requires a human presence. The work occurs in highly controlled clinical environments, but the necessity for human moral authority and the legal accountability for clinical decision-making remain significant barriers to full automation.

rows.title: Historians
rows.old_rationale: Historians perform high-level knowledge work—researching, analyzing, and writing—that is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains in document processing and synthesis. While the job requires human judgment and physical interaction with artifacts or archives, the core output of reports and articles faces significant restructuring as AI tools become capable of parsing vast historical datasets and drafting complex narratives.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a historian—written reports, digital archives, and interpretive narratives—is highly amenable to AI generation, as the primary task is the synthesis of existing data. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to interpret historical nuance, the physical environment is largely controlled (archives, offices), and the role lacks the high-stakes, real-time systemic accountability or physical necessity that would protect it from automation.

rows.title: Military careers
rows.old_rationale: Military careers are a vast composite of physical combat, manual labor, and high-level digital strategy. While AI will heavily automate back-office administration, logistics, and intelligence analysis (high exposure), the core requirements of physical presence, tactical human leadership, and operation in unpredictable physical environments provide a significant buffer.
rows.new_rationale: While military operations increasingly utilize digital and autonomous systems, the core output remains physical, kinetic, and geopolitical, requiring human presence in highly volatile, unstructured environments. The necessity for human moral judgment, ethical accountability in the use of force, and the legal weight of command decisions makes this occupation fundamentally resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Home health and personal care aides
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical and interpersonal, involving manual tasks like bathing, dressing, and lifting patients in unpredictable home environments. While AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring vital signs, it cannot replace the essential human presence and physical labor required for personal care.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in physical, empathetic, and trust-based human interaction that cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While some tasks are routine, the work occurs in highly unstructured, private home environments where the aide must navigate unpredictable physical and emotional variables, making the human presence an ontological necessity rather than a functional one.

rows.title: Top executives
rows.old_rationale: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While executive strategy and resource allocation are increasingly data-driven and susceptible to AI-assisted execution, the role remains anchored in human-centric leadership, negotiation, and the assumption of ultimate legal and moral liability. The necessity for a human 'buck-stops-here' figure to navigate organizational politics and maintain stakeholder trust creates a high barrier to full automation, despite the high potential for AI to handle the underlying analytical synthesis.

rows.title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—scheduling, drafting correspondence, managing databases, and transcribing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of LLMs and AI agents. While some roles require physical presence for visitor greeting or office management, the vast majority of tasks are routine information processing that can be automated or significantly streamlined, leading to major restructuring and reduced headcount per organization.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by secretaries—scheduling, document preparation, and data entry—are inherently digital and highly susceptible to automation by agentic software. Because the work occurs in highly structured office environments and relies on standardized procedures, there is minimal physical unpredictability or requirement for human-to-human emotional labor that an AI cannot simulate or replace.

rows.title: Registered nurses
rows.old_rationale: Registered nursing is a hybrid role that combines significant physical labor, such as administering treatments and moving patients, with complex interpersonal care and emotional support. While AI will heavily impact the knowledge-work components—such as patient monitoring, diagnostic analysis, and care planning—the core requirement for physical presence and human empathy creates a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data synthesis and monitoring, the core of nursing involves high-stakes physical interventions in unpredictable, non-standardized environments (patient bodies and bedside settings) that require real-time, nuanced physical dexterity. Furthermore, the role is deeply anchored in human-to-human emotional labor and non-delegatable legal and ethical accountability for life-critical decisions, making full automation of the occupation impossible.

rows.title: Cashiers
rows.old_rationale: While the job has physical components like bagging and stocking, the core function of processing transactions is highly susceptible to automation through computer vision and AI-driven self-checkout systems. The BLS already projects a significant decline in employment due to these technological shifts, as AI can increasingly handle age verification, fraud detection, and customer inquiries.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of a cashier—processing transactions, scanning items, and managing payments—are almost entirely digital or automated, making them highly susceptible to AI and self-service technology. While there is a minor human element in customer service and age verification, these tasks are increasingly handled by software or simple physical sensors, leaving little room for complex cognitive synthesis or non-delegatable human accountability.

rows.title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and test automation being primary use cases for Large Language Models. While high-level system architecture and complex stakeholder communication provide some insulation, AI is drastically increasing individual productivity, which will likely lead to significant restructuring of entry-level roles and QA functions.
rows.new_rationale: Software development is a purely digital discipline where the output is code that can be autonomously executed, tested, and deployed by AI agents. While the work requires high-level architectural synthesis and carries significant systemic accountability for security and system stability, it occurs in a highly controlled, virtual environment that lacks the physical unpredictability of manual labor.

rows.title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—negotiating contracts, processing orders, and managing client relationships—is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. While the role requires human-to-human trust and persuasion to close high-stakes deals, the actual execution of sales, reporting, and administrative coordination is shifting toward software agents that can operate within the relatively structured environment of B2B commerce.

rows.title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of nursing assistants and orderlies—such as bathing, dressing, and physically transferring patients—are highly manual and require real-time human presence in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like recording vital signs or scheduling, the primary work product is physical care and interpersonal support, which remains largely insulated from AI automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in physical, high-stakes interaction with human bodies in unstructured, unpredictable environments where mechanical automation struggles with delicate, non-standardized tasks. Furthermore, the role requires a profound level of human presence, empathy, and moral accountability for patient well-being that cannot be offloaded to a digital agent without fundamentally degrading the quality of care.

rows.title: Postsecondary teachers
rows.old_rationale: Postsecondary teaching is primarily knowledge work involving research, writing, and curriculum design—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human interaction and mentorship, AI can automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews, potentially increasing individual productivity to the point of restructuring faculty needs.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—instruction, curriculum design, and research—is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven generation and delivery. While the environment is highly controlled, the role retains a high score for human necessity because the pedagogical value is deeply rooted in mentorship, social modeling, and the human-to-human bond, which remains a critical component of the educational product.

rows.title: Information clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—processing routine data, answering inquiries, and maintaining records—are fundamentally digital and information-based. While some roles like hotel desk clerks or airport agents require a physical presence, the majority of the work involves routine information processing that is increasingly being automated by AI-driven self-service systems, chatbots, and automated data entry tools.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by information clerks—data entry, record retrieval, and processing standard forms—are inherently digital and highly susceptible to automation by software agents. While some roles involve physical interaction or customer service, the core value is informational and procedural, requiring minimal complex synthesis and occurring in highly controlled, predictable office or service environments.

rows.title: Material recording clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—tracking data, scheduling, and inventory reporting—are digital information-processing tasks that AI and automated systems excel at. While some physical presence is required to inspect goods or handle packages, the rapid integration of AI-driven logistics software, RFID technology, and computer vision for quality control is already leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of this role—tracking, recording, and reporting inventory—are almost entirely digital and already being subsumed by RFID and automated warehouse management systems. While the work occurs in physical warehouses, the tasks are highly structured and repetitive, requiring minimal complex synthesis or human-to-human emotional labor, making them prime candidates for full automation.

rows.title: Management analysts
rows.old_rationale: Management analysts perform high-level knowledge work—including data analysis, report writing, and financial modeling—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills for client interviews and stakeholder management, the core 'back-office' tasks of synthesizing information and developing efficiency recommendations are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a management analyst is a digital artifact—a report, strategy, or system recommendation—which is highly susceptible to AI automation. While the cognitive synthesis required to navigate organizational politics and unique business challenges is high, the human necessity remains significant primarily for the purpose of building trust, managing stakeholder buy-in, and assuming the professional liability for high-stakes corporate restructuring.

rows.title: Project management specialists
rows.old_rationale: Project management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation involving data analysis, scheduling, and documentation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While AI can significantly increase productivity in planning and monitoring, the role's heavy reliance on human-centric skills like stakeholder negotiation, conflict resolution, and team leadership provides a protective barrier against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Project management is highly susceptible to automation because the primary outputs—schedules, budgets, and status reports—are digital artifacts that AI agents can generate and execute. While the role requires high-level synthesis and human-centric negotiation to manage stakeholder expectations and team dynamics, the core workflow is increasingly data-driven and can be managed by software, leaving the human primarily responsible for the ultimate accountability and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution.

rows.title: Receptionists
rows.old_rationale: Many core tasks such as scheduling, answering phones, and data entry are highly susceptible to automation via AI voice agents and chatbots. While the role retains a physical component for greeting and security, the BLS already projects zero growth due to automation, and the digital nature of the administrative workload makes it highly exposed to further restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of receptionist tasks—scheduling, data entry, and information routing—are digital artifacts that can be fully executed by autonomous agents or integrated software systems. While there is a minor requirement for human presence in physical greeting and security, the core functions are highly susceptible to automation due to their structured nature and low requirement for complex, non-routine cognitive synthesis.

rows.title: Childcare workers
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of this role—physical care, safety monitoring, hygiene, and emotional support—require a physical presence and human empathy that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, record-keeping, or parent communication, the fundamental nature of the work is hands-on and occurs in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: Childcare is fundamentally anchored in the physical, emotional, and safety-critical needs of human children, which cannot be delegated to digital agents. While AI can assist with scheduling or curriculum planning, the core value—providing physical care, emotional regulation, and immediate safety in an unpredictable, high-stakes environment—requires a human presence that carries full moral and legal accountability.

rows.title: Human resources specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of recruiting, screening, and administrative record-keeping are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime targets for AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction and human judgment for interviewing and employee relations, AI can now handle complex scheduling, initial candidate vetting, and policy inquiries, significantly increasing individual worker productivity and reducing the need for entry-level administrative staff.
rows.new_rationale: The majority of HR tasks involve digital artifacts like records, payroll, and communication, making them highly susceptible to agentic automation. However, the role retains a high ontological necessity score because the core functions—conflict resolution, employee relations, and nuanced interpersonal judgment—rely on human empathy and the perception of fairness that AI currently struggles to replicate authentically.

rows.title: Farmers, ranchers, and other agricultural managers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, equipment maintenance, and real-time management of biological systems that cannot be digitized. However, AI will heavily impact the management side of the job, including crop yield prediction, financial planning, and automated resource allocation, making individual managers significantly more productive and contributing to further farm consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural management involves complex data-driven decision-making that AI can augment, the final output is tied to biological and physical systems that are highly volatile and unstructured. The necessity for human intervention remains high due to the unpredictable nature of weather, disease, and physical maintenance in non-standardized environments, limiting the ability of purely digital agents to execute the full lifecycle of production.

rows.title: Agricultural workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, animal handling, and operating machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier to AI. While AI and robotics are increasingly used for precision agriculture and automated harvesting, the high cost of hardware and the need for manual dexterity in varied conditions mean human workers remain essential for the majority of tasks.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural work is increasingly mechanized, the core value remains physical interaction with highly variable, unstructured biological environments (weather, soil conditions, animal behavior) that are difficult for current AI agents to navigate autonomously. The output is primarily physical—crops harvested or animals tended—rather than digital, and the role lacks the high-stakes, non-delegatable legal liability found in professional services.

rows.title: Medical assistants
rows.old_rationale: Medical assistants perform a hybrid of physical and digital tasks; while AI can significantly automate administrative duties like scheduling, coding, and medical record entry, it cannot perform physical clinical tasks such as drawing blood, administering injections, or taking manual vital signs. The role requires a high degree of real-time human interaction and empathy in a physical clinical setting, which serves as a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks and data entry have high agentic potential, the core clinical duties involve physical interaction with patients and biological specimens in a clinical setting, which limits full automation. The high score for ontological human necessity reflects the critical role of empathy and human presence in patient care, which remains a primary value proposition that software cannot replicate.

rows.title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time mechanical repair in a physical environment, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance software, the actual physical replacement of parts and hands-on troubleshooting remains a human-centric task.
rows.new_rationale: While diagnostic software can identify issues, the physical execution of repairs requires high-dexterity manipulation of heavy, non-standardized mechanical components in cramped, unpredictable environments that remain difficult for current robotics. Furthermore, the role carries high systemic accountability, as improper repairs to safety-critical systems like brakes or steering pose immediate, life-altering risks that require human professional liability.

rows.title: Sales managers
rows.old_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly susceptible to AI because the primary outputs—sales forecasting, CRM management, and performance monitoring—are digital artifacts that can be automated or executed by agents. While the work involves complex strategy, the core value remains heavily tied to human-to-human motivation, leadership, and the negotiation of trust, which limits full automation despite the high potential for digital execution of tactical tasks.

rows.title: Medical and health services managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a high volume of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core responsibilities of recruiting, supervising staff, and navigating complex interpersonal relationships within a physical healthcare facility provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative, financial, and scheduling output can be automated by AI agents, the role requires high-level synthesis of regulatory, clinical, and human resource variables. The necessity for human leadership in high-stakes, life-critical healthcare environments and the non-delegatable legal liability for patient outcomes and facility compliance ensure that human oversight remains essential.

rows.title: Insurance sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing policies, customizing insurance programs, and maintaining records are digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal trust and persuasive sales skills, AI-powered chatbots and recommendation engines are increasingly capable of handling routine inquiries and policy comparisons, significantly increasing individual agent productivity and reducing the need for human intervention in simple transactions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—policy selection, data entry, and contract execution—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by AI agents. While the job requires human-to-human trust and legal accountability for advice given, the actual synthesis of financial data and the execution of sales are increasingly functional tasks that do not require physical presence or unstructured environmental navigation.

rows.title: Construction equipment operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, outdoor environments that require real-time human coordination and manual maintenance. While AI and automation are increasingly integrated into equipment via GPS and computerized controls, the physical nature of the work and the need for on-site safety judgment provide a significant barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: While the operation of machinery is increasingly automated, the core value of this role lies in navigating highly unstructured, volatile physical environments where real-time sensory judgment is critical for safety. The low agentic potential reflects the fact that the output is a physical modification of the earth, which requires complex, non-delegatable accountability for life-altering safety risks on a job site.

rows.title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time troubleshooting in unpredictable physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance scheduling, the actual repair, installation, and physical manipulation of heavy machinery still require human presence and mechanical skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical intervention in unstructured, high-stakes industrial environments, which remains highly resistant to autonomous agents due to the need for complex tactile manipulation and real-time physical problem-solving. While diagnostic tasks are increasingly digital, the final output—repairing, moving, or installing heavy machinery—requires physical presence and accountability for safety-critical outcomes that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
rows.old_rationale: Real estate agents perform a mix of digital knowledge work and physical/interpersonal tasks. AI can automate significant portions of their digital workload, such as market analysis, property descriptions, lead generation, and contract preparation, which increases individual productivity and may reduce the total number of agents needed. However, the core of the job still requires physical property showings, high-stakes human negotiation, and local networking, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and informational work (listing, document preparation, market analysis) is highly susceptible to agentic automation, the role remains anchored in high-stakes human negotiation and the necessity of building trust in a major life-altering transaction. The 'human-in-the-loop' is essential not just for the physical showing of properties, but for the emotional mediation and legal liability inherent in representing clients during significant financial commitments.

rows.title: Computer systems analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving data modeling, system design, and technical analysis that can be performed entirely on a computer. AI is highly capable of automating core tasks such as writing technical documentation, generating system diagrams, analyzing cost-benefit data, and assisting with the programming and debugging aspects of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital architecture and system design, which is highly susceptible to automation by advanced AI agents capable of end-to-end configuration and deployment. While the job requires high-level synthesis of business needs and technical constraints, the work occurs in highly controlled, virtual environments, leaving little room for the physical unpredictability that protects other roles from automation.

rows.title: Animal care and service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are physical and require real-time presence, such as feeding, grooming, cleaning, and physically training animals. AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring animal health data, but it cannot replace the manual labor or the empathetic human-animal interaction required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is rooted in physical interaction with living, unpredictable organisms, which requires real-time, embodied responses that software cannot currently execute. While some administrative tasks are digitizable, the primary work involves physical labor, safety management in volatile environments, and the human-centric provision of care and empathy that remains essential to the service.

rows.title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and information-based, involving market research, data analysis, and content strategy—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While high-level strategic decision-making and stakeholder management provide some insulation, AI can now automate significant portions of campaign planning, creative drafting, and consumer data processing, leading to major restructuring of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this role is almost entirely digital—campaign strategies, media buys, and content assets—which are highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents.
